column3: question
column4: choices
column6: explanation

column3: A UX Designer is tasked with ensuring Lightning App Builder apps are mobile-friendly, including interactive elements.What should be the minimum touch screen target size for interactive elements on mobile devices?
column4: A. 24 pixels wide x 24 pixels tall | B. 44 pixels wide x 44 pixels tall | C. 64 pixels wide x 64 pixels tall
column6: The 44x44 pixel minimum touch target size is the industry standard established by Apple's Human Interface Guidelines and echoed by WCAG 2.5.5 (AAA) and Salesforce's own mobile design guidelines. This size accommodates the average human fingertip contact area, reducing tap errors on touchscreens. 24px is too small and causes frequent mis-taps, while 64px is unnecessarily large and wastes screen real estate on mobile devices.

column3: Cloud Kicks needs functional components that will be used on many pages. The components need to be consistent with the look and feel of Lightning Experience.What does the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) provide that will ensure consistency?
column4: A. Experience Lightning Components | B. ComponentBlueprints | C. JavaScript Frameworks | D. Custom Lightning components
column6: SLDS Component Blueprints are HTML/CSS templates that define the visual structure and styling of UI components to match Lightning Experience's look and feel. They provide the CSS classes, markup patterns, and design tokens needed to build consistent interfaces without relying on JavaScript frameworks. Experience Lightning Components (A) and Custom Lightning components (D) are implementation choices, while JavaScript Frameworks (C) are not part of SLDS.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) allows its partners to manage leads and opportunities. CK's relationship manager has requested that partners are able to quickly browse and see their opportunities segmented by key attributes:If the opportunity is closing within 2 weeks If it is valued at more than $100,000 Which approach should be used in Experience Builder to enable this?
column4: A. Create a custom Lightning Web Component using a data table for each key attribute. | B. Allow each user to create a filter for each key attribute and link to the opportunities list view. | C. Configure a pre-filtered opportunity list view for each key attribute with the partner group.
column6: Pre-configured list views shared with partner groups provide a guided, low-friction browsing experience without requiring users to build their own filters. This is the declarative, scalable approach in Experience Builder — an admin creates the filtered list views once and shares them with the appropriate partner community group. Option B places burden on end users who may lack skills or time, and option A requires custom development when a no-code solution exists.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to modify one of its custom Lightning Web Components so that its administrators can change the look and feel depending on what type of Lightning page is used in.Which feature should be recommended?
column4: A. Styling hooks | B. CSS loaded as a static resource | C. App Builder styling property | D. SLDS utility classes
column6: App Builder styling properties (also called Design Attributes or targetConfig properties) allow administrators to configure a component's appearance directly within Lightning App Builder without writing code. This gives admins a declarative way to adjust the look and feel per page context. Styling hooks (A) and SLDS utility classes (D) require developer intervention, and loading CSS as a static resource (B) is not an admin-friendly approach.

column3: When designing a custom component that includes this clickable icon in a Lookup field:What should be used as the alternative (alt) text?
column4: A. Image of a search button | B. Search | C. Search button | D. Image of a magnifying glass
column6: For interactive/clickable icons, the alt text should describe the function of the element, not its visual appearance. "Search button" communicates both the action (search) and the role (button) to screen reader users, providing complete context. "Search" alone (B) omits the interactive role, while "Image of a search button" (A) and "Image of a magnifying glass" (D) describe the visual rather than the function, which is poor accessibility practice.

column3: Cloud Kicks marketing development representatives need to process incoming leads. Understanding the typical lead to opportunity is essential to the design.Which three new records would typically be created when they convert a lead?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Contact | B. Activity | C. Converted Lead | D. Account | E. Opportunity
column6: When a Lead is converted in Salesforce, the system creates three new records: a Contact (person), an Account (company), and an Opportunity (potential deal). This is standard Salesforce lead conversion behavior. Activities (B) are not automatically created during conversion, and "Converted Lead" (C) is not a separate object — the original Lead record is simply marked as converted.

column3: A UX Designer has been asked to optimize a Lightning app for screen reader-assisted accessibility.How should the page be optimized?
column4: A. Switch to a Lightning Console layout. | B. Place components in adjacent tabs. | C. Stack components on the same page. | D. Use accordions to organize components.
column6: Accordions allow content to be logically organized and collapsed/expanded on a single page, which works well with screen readers because the structure is sequential and each section has a clear heading. Screen readers navigate linearly, so accordions on one page are easier to traverse than tabbed navigation (B) which requires managing focus across tab panels, or console layouts (A) which have complex multi-pane structures. Stacking all components (C) without structure creates an overwhelming wall of content for screen reader users.

column3: Which feature is provided in the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) for designers and developers?
column4: A. A library of Lightning Web Components | B. Semantic and accessible component markup | C. ES6 JavaScript modules and plugins
column6: SLDS provides semantic HTML markup patterns and accessible component blueprints that follow WCAG guidelines, ensuring consistent and accessible UI across Salesforce applications. SLDS is a CSS framework and design system — it does not provide a library of functional LWC components (A, which is the LWC library itself) or JavaScript modules and plugins (C, which are not part of SLDS).

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks is planning out the company's website redesign using Experience Cloud and will help guide discovery.Which three outputs of the discovery process should be prepared? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. User Personas | B. Journey Maps | C. interactive Prototypes | D. High-Level Wireframes | E. High-Fidelity Mock-ups
column6: The discovery phase focuses on understanding users and defining the problem space, so its outputs are User Personas (representing target audiences), Journey Maps (showing current-state experiences and pain points), and High-Level Wireframes (rough structural layouts to validate direction). Interactive Prototypes (C) and High-Fidelity Mock-ups (E) are outputs of later design and prototyping phases, not discovery.

column3: A UX Designer wants to ensure new Salesforce users are given the appropriate onboarding experience.Which two on-App Guidance customizations should be used?
column4: A. Specify prompt scheduling | B. Customize prompt theme | C. Set prompt permissions | D. Configure minimum three steps
column6: In-App Guidance prompts support two key customizations for appropriate onboarding: scheduling (A) lets you control when prompts appear (e.g., only for new users or after a feature release), and permissions (C) let you target specific user profiles or permission sets so only the right users see the guidance. Customizing prompt theme (B) is a cosmetic option and not essential to appropriate onboarding targeting, and "minimum three steps" (D) is not a real In-App Guidance configuration option.

column3: Cloud Kicks hired a UX Designer to help create a form for a wide group of users. After receiving that final requirement, the designer realizes there are too many fields.What could improve form readability?
column4: A. Improve form security by adding a challenge-response test. | B. Create a three-column grid to reduce the form length. | C. Replace field labels with placeholder text. | D. Add section headers to visually separate fields into groups.
column6: Section headers group related fields together, creating visual hierarchy that makes long forms easier to scan and understand. This is a standard UX best practice for improving form readability. A three-column grid (B) actually increases cognitive load and causes misalignment issues. Replacing labels with placeholder text (C) reduces accessibility and usability since placeholders disappear when users type. Adding a CAPTCHA (A) adds friction and does nothing for readability.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) uses a custom lightning component with an Apex class to display shipment information (custom object, private OWD). UC sales managers are complaining about two important points:Shipment records that belong to their teams can be seen by other users.Shipment amount should be visible only by managers, but sales reps are able to view it.Which two features did the development team miss that is causing the problems?Choose 2 answers.
column4: A. Use runAs in test class to enforce user permissions and field-level permissions. | B. Use With Sharing keyword in Apex classes to enforce sharing rules evaluation. | C. Use isSharable keyword in Apex classes to assurerecord visibility. | D. Use isAccessible() method in Apex classes to check field accessibility
column6: The two problems correspond to two distinct security layers: record-level visibility and field-level security. Using "with sharing" in the Apex class (B) ensures that Salesforce's sharing rules and OWD settings are respected when querying records, preventing users from seeing records they shouldn't. Using the isAccessible() method (D) from Schema.DescribeFieldResult enforces field-level security before displaying field values. runAs (A) is only for test classes and doesn't affect production, and isSharable (C) is not a real Salesforce keyword.

column3: Cloud Kicks's development team is working on the build of a new custom component using VS Code. They often have new CSS classes and properties conflicting with the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS).What should simplify working with SLDS in Lightning Components?
column4: A. Salesforce Developer Console should be used instead | B. SLDS Validator extension for VS Code | C. Install SLDS creator from AppExchange | D. Keep the SLDS Stylesheet open for reference
column6: The SLDS Validator is a free VS Code extension published by Salesforce that provides real-time linting, autocompletion, and validation of SLDS CSS classes within Lightning components. It directly addresses CSS class conflicts by flagging incorrect or deprecated SLDS class usage as developers type. The Developer Console (A) offers no SLDS-specific assistance, there is no "SLDS creator" on AppExchange (C), and keeping the stylesheet open (D) is a manual, error-prone process.

column3: A UX Designer wants to build on a human-centered design by focusing on more than just an individual person and is considering engaging, connected, and social value-driven solutions.What is the designer practicing?
column4: A. Compassionate Design | B. Relationship Design | C. Service Design | D. User Experience Design
column6: Service Design takes a holistic systems-level view, considering not just the individual user but the entire ecosystem of people, processes, and touchpoints that make up a service experience. It focuses on connected, social, and value-driven solutions across an entire journey. Compassionate Design (A) is about empathy for individual users, Relationship Design (B) focuses on interpersonal connections, and UX Design (D) typically centers on individual user interactions with a product.

column3: A UX Designer is customizing the look and feel of a site using Experience Builder and needs to choose a color for the text and the background of the buttons.Which accessibility guideline should be considered?
column4: A. Adaptable | B. Distinguishable | C. Navigable
column6: "Distinguishable" is WCAG Principle 1.4, which covers the use of color and contrast to make content easier to see and hear. It includes Success Criteria 1.4.3 (minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text) and 1.4.11 (3:1 for UI components), directly applicable to button text/background color choices. Adaptable (A) relates to content that can be presented in different ways, and Navigable (C) relates to keyboard navigation and focus management.

column3: Users from a small group within a Sales team have complained about an object that is often usedonly by team that has not been added to their Lightning app. Due to the small volume of users, the administrator is not considering creating a new app for them.Which two Salesforce feature should be suggested to improve the end-user experience?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Favorite the often-used object. | B. Add the object's related list to the Home page. | C. Personalize the navigation bar. | D. Create a custom component on a Dashboard.
column6: Both Favorites (A) and personalizing the navigation bar (C) are user-driven, no-admin-required solutions that give individual users quick access to objects not in their app. Users can star/favorite any record, list view, or object and access it from the Favorites menu; they can also add items to their personal navigation bar. Adding a related list to the Home page (B) doesn't give access to the object itself, and a Dashboard component (D) is indirect and limited.

column3: During user interviews, a UX Designer discovers that the most common daily task for users is to look for and view commonly used reportsusing the global search bar.What should be done to improve their experience?
column4: A. Make the global search bar bigger on every page. | B. Update the Home page with access to commonly used reports. | C. Add the Daily Task component to the Home page. | D. Create a mood board to communicate the visual style of the UI.
column6: Since the primary daily task is accessing specific reports, the most efficient UX solution is to surface those reports directly on the Home page, eliminating the need to search at all. This reduces friction by converting a multi-step search process into a single-click access. Making the search bar bigger (A) doesn't reduce the number of steps, the Daily Task component (C) is for activities/tasks not reports, and a mood board (D) is a visual design artifact unrelated to navigation efficiency.

column3: A company provides a way for customers to shop for homes and contact real estate agents online. The company's brokers use some of the Salesforce standard functionality to track home buyers.Which three standard Salesforce objects should be used in this experience?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Property | B. Lead | C. Contact | D. Opportunity | E. Address
column6: For a real estate CRM use case: Leads (B) represent prospective home buyers who have expressed interest, Contacts (C) represent known buyers with established relationships, and Opportunities (D) represent specific deals (e.g., a buyer pursuing a particular property). Property (A) and Address (E) are not standard Salesforce objects — Property would need to be a custom object, and Address is a compound field, not a standalone object.

column3: Which part of the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) should be used to create visual consistency with regard to alignments, grids, spacing, and typography'
column4: A. Component Blueprints | B. BEM Naming Classes | C. Utility Classes
column6: SLDS Utility Classes are single-purpose CSS classes for layout (grid system, alignment), spacing (padding/margin), typography (font sizes, weights), and other common visual properties. They allow developers to apply consistent styling directly in HTML without writing custom CSS. Component Blueprints (A) define the structure of specific UI components, and BEM naming (B) is a CSS naming methodology, not a set of layout utility classes.

column3: What are Salesforce core design principles when making design decisions?
column4: A. Emphasis, Alignment. Consistency, Beauty | B. Clarity, Efficiency, Consistency, Beauty | C. Emphasis, Efficiency, Repetition, Proportion | D. Clarity, Efficiency, Balance, Proportion
column6: Salesforce's four core design principles are Clarity (making the interface understandable), Efficiency (helping users accomplish tasks quickly), Consistency (creating familiar, predictable experiences), and Beauty (creating visually pleasing, well-crafted interfaces). These principles are documented in the Salesforce Lightning Design System and guide all design decisions across Lightning Experience and Experience Cloud.

column3: How would a UX Designer Differentiate between voice and tone?
column4: A. Voice reflects the expression and tone is the way one designs. | B. Voice reflects the character and tone is one's strength. | C. Voice reflects the frequency and tone is one's pitch. | D. Voice reflects the personality and tone is the way one speaks.
column6: In UX writing and content design, voice is the consistent personality and character of a brand that remains constant (who you are), while tone is the way that personality is expressed or modulated depending on context and situation (how you speak in a given moment). For example, a brand might always be friendly (voice) but adjust from formal to casual depending on whether the context is an error message or a success celebration (tone).

column3: What are three benefits of asking the user to think aloud during a user task? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. No special equipment needed | B. Natural process | C. Unbiased behavior | D. Flexibility | E. Raw stream of thought
column6: The think-aloud protocol benefits include: no special equipment (A) — only an observer and recording device are needed; flexibility (D) — sessions can be adapted in real-time based on what users say; and raw stream of thought (E) — it surfaces unfiltered mental models, confusion points, and decision-making in real time. Think-aloud is not a natural process (B) since verbalizing while doing a task is artificially imposed, and it is subject to bias (C) since participants may alter behavior knowing they are being observed and listened to.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to provide employees with quick access to apps, objects, and other items in the Salesforce mobile app. Which navigational feature should CK use?
column4: A. Personalized Navigation Tabs | B. Console Navigation | C. Utility Bar
column6: Personalized Navigation Tabs in the Salesforce mobile app allow users (or admins) to configure which apps, objects, and items appear in the bottom navigation bar for quick access. Console Navigation (B) is a desktop-only feature for multi-tab workspace layouts and is not available in the Salesforce mobile app. The Utility Bar (C) is also a desktop Lightning app feature and is not supported in the mobile app.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants its Salesforce app to represent company branding and comply with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1.What should they consider about themes and branding?
column4: A. Salesforce automatically updates the selected brand color to make it accessible; this should overridden to select the colors manually. | B. There is a default WCAG compliant Salesforce color theme; only brand color can be updated. | C. The color palette is auto-generated based on the brand color to be WCAG compliant.
column6: When you set a brand color in Salesforce's Themes and Branding feature, Salesforce automatically generates an accessible color palette derived from that brand color, ensuring the resulting theme meets WCAG contrast requirements. This means organizations do not need to manually validate every color combination — the system handles accessibility compliance through algorithmic palette generation. This makes it easier to maintain branding and accessibility simultaneously without custom CSS overrides.

column3: Cloud Kicks hired a UX Designer to help improve the design of its Experience Cloud site. The company knows the interface needs updating but is unsure about what should be done.Which tool should the designer utilize first?
column4: A. Heuristic Evaluation | B. Low-Fidelity Prototype | C. Design Template
column6: A Heuristic Evaluation is the right first step when the problem is undefined — it is an expert review of the existing interface against established usability principles (e.g., Nielsen's 10 heuristics) that quickly identifies usability issues without requiring user recruitment. This provides a baseline understanding of what needs fixing before designing solutions. A Low-Fidelity Prototype (B) is for exploring solutions, not diagnosing problems, and a Design Template (C) is an implementation tool, not a research method.

column3: Cloud Kicks is launching a new Salesforce org and wants to test its levels of accessibility, including keyboard navigation.Which detail could be verified by testing keyboard navigation for accessibility?
column4: A. Tabbing order is logical. | B. Keyboard actions provide audio feedback. | C. Actionable items are highlighted in a specific color.
column6: Keyboard navigation testing specifically verifies that users can navigate through all interactive elements in a logical, meaningful order using the Tab key — this is WCAG Success Criterion 2.4.3 (Focus Order). Audio feedback (B) is related to screen readers and audio assistive technology, not keyboard navigation testing per se. Color highlighting of actionable items (C) relates to visual focus indicators, which is a separate but related accessibility concern (WCAG 2.4.7).

column3: What is a benefit of inclusive design?
column4: A. Reducing friction for users in achieving their goals | B. Tailoring a solution to one type of user | C. Creating a lowest-common-denominator design
column6: Inclusive design aims to create products that work for the widest possible range of people, which inherently means removing barriers and reducing friction for all users — including those with disabilities, diverse backgrounds, or varying technical abilities. Tailoring solutions to one type of user (B) is the opposite of inclusive design. Creating a lowest-common-denominator design (C) is a common misconception; inclusive design actually raises the quality for everyone by solving for edge cases that reveal better overall solutions.

column3: How should a UX designer differentiate between a voice and a tone?
column4: A. Voice reflects the expression and the tone is the way one designs | B. Voice reflects the character and tone is one's strength | C. Voice reflects the frequency and tone is one's pitch | D. Voice reflects the personality and tone is the way ones speaks
column6: Voice is the consistent personality of a brand — it stays the same regardless of context (what you are). Tone is how that personality is expressed in a given situation — it adapts based on context, audience, and emotional register (how you say it). This question is a duplicate of #21, and the answer and concept remain the same: voice = personality, tone = manner of speaking.

column3: A UX Designer wants to communicate the value of diversity, inclusion, and equality in design.Which business outcomes represent these values?
column4: A. Inclusive recruitment, lower market share, and less employee turnover | B. Economic growth, greater market share, and less employee turnover | C. Critical investing, more employee turnover, and greater market share
column6: Research consistently shows that diverse and inclusive organizations achieve better business outcomes: economic growth from broader market reach and better innovation, greater market share because products designed inclusively serve larger and more varied audiences, and less employee turnover because inclusive cultures improve employee satisfaction and retention. Options A and C include clearly negative outcomes (lower market share, more employee turnover) that contradict the business case for diversity and inclusion.

column3: During our interview, a UX designer discovers that the most common daily task for the user is to view and commonly view reports using the global search bar for:
column4: A. Make the global search bar bigger on every page | B. Update the homepage with access to commonly used reports | C. Add the daily task component to the homepage | D. Create the mood board to communicate the visual style of the UI
column6: This is a duplicate of question #17. When users' primary daily task is finding and viewing specific reports, the best UX solution is to eliminate the search step entirely by placing direct access to those reports on the Home page. This is the principle of progressive disclosure and reducing interaction cost — surfacing the most-used content at the point of entry saves multiple steps per session.

column3: The service team at Cloud Kicks has complained about the quantity of list views available, ,making it hard find the relevant ones.In which two ways should their experience be improved? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Request users to create and share their list views. | B. Share list views to Public Groups and only add relevant users. | C. Remove irrelevant public list views. | D. Recommend using related lists instead of list views.
column6: The two best approaches to reduce list view clutter are: sharing list views only to relevant Public Groups (B) so team members only see views applicable to their role, and removing irrelevant public list views (C) that contribute to the noise. Asking users to create and share their own views (A) adds to the problem rather than solving it. Switching to related lists (D) serves a different purpose and doesn't address the core issue of too many list views.

column3: Cloud Kicks has asked its UX Designer to optimize Salesforce to help the IT help desk team quickly resolve the team's queued Cases.What Salesforce-based approach should be recommended to quickly show the appropriate cases to IT help desk team members?
column4: A. Add a Case Assignment component to the team's home page configured to shown cases assigned to the logged in team member. | B. Add a List View component to the home page configured to show a custom list view filtered to show assigned and open cases. | C. Add a Case inbox component to the page configured to show assigned and open cases.
column6: Adding a List View component to the Home page with a pre-configured filter showing assigned, open cases is the standard, declarative Salesforce approach that gives agents immediate visibility into their work queue upon login. Salesforce does not have a standard "Case Assignment component" (A) with that exact functionality, and while Case Inbox exists for email-to-case, it is not the same as a general queued case view (C). The List View component is a native, flexible solution that works out of the box.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has hired a UX Designer to help with the design of its Experience Cloud site. CK wants to understand the structure and layout of the navigation menu.Which activity should the designer use while card sorting?
column4: A. Compare two versions of the navigation menu mockups to see which performs better. | B. Have users sort the cards based on similar categories or groups. | C. Arrange pages in the navigation menu by alphabetical order.
column6: Card sorting is a user research method where participants organize topic cards into groups that make sense to them, revealing their mental models for information architecture. Having users sort cards into categories (B) is the core definition of card sorting. Comparing two versions of mockups (A) describes A/B testing or comparative usability testing. Alphabetical arrangement (C) is an arbitrary organizational strategy, not a research method.

column3: A developer is creating a Lightning Web Component (LWC) and wants to make sure the visual experience is consistent with Cloud Kicks' branding. The developer asks their UX Designer about the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) styling hooks.How should the designer describe them?
column4: A. They use standard CSS properties to directly style HTML elements. | B. They use standard CSS properties to easily style base and custom components. | C. They use custom CSS properties to directly style HTML elements. | D. The use custom CSS properties to easily style case and custom components.
column6: SLDS styling hooks are CSS custom properties (CSS variables, which are "custom" properties prefixed with --) that expose specific styling points on base components, allowing developers to adjust visual aspects like color, spacing, and typography without modifying the component's internal shadow DOM. They target base and custom components (not raw HTML elements directly), making option D correct. Options A and B incorrectly describe them as standard CSS properties rather than custom CSS properties.

column3: Which two consideration should be made when conducting a Consequence Scanning workshop?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Consider design ramifications to prevent misuse and protect communities. | B. Prioritize marketing opportunities when designing features of a product. | C. Bring together a cross-function group with varied experiences. | D. Examine a product for potential inclusivity after it has been built
column6: A Consequence Scanning workshop is an ethical design practice that anticipates intended and unintended consequences of a product. Two key considerations are: examining design ramifications to prevent misuse and protect communities (A) — identifying potential harms before they occur, and bringing together a cross-functional group with varied experiences (C) — diverse perspectives surface blind spots. Marketing prioritization (B) is not the purpose of Consequence Scanning, and examining inclusivity after building (D) is too late and contradicts the proactive nature of the workshop.

column3: Cloud Kicks asks its UX Designer to create a B2B sales portal that can easily integrate customer relationship management.Which Salesforce solution should be used?
column4: A. Commerce Cloud | B. Experience Cloud | C. Sales Cloud
column6: Experience Cloud (formerly Community Cloud) is Salesforce's platform for building external portals and sites, including B2B partner portals that integrate natively with Sales Cloud CRM data. It provides templates specifically for partner and customer communities with built-in CRM integration. Commerce Cloud (A) is for e-commerce transactions. Sales Cloud (C) is the internal CRM platform for sales teams, not an external-facing portal builder.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to hire a deal closer.Which three activities would someone in this role do each day?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Update existing prospect and customer records. | B. Log activities such as calls, emails, and notes. | C. Review and work through their list of leads or opportunities. | D. Create cases from issues. | E. Customize and administer Salesforce.
column6: A deal closer (sales representative) focuses daily on: updating prospect/customer records (A) to keep CRM data current, logging activities like calls, emails, and notes (B) to track interactions, and working through their pipeline of leads or opportunities (C) to advance deals to close. Creating cases from issues (D) is a service/support function, and customizing/administering Salesforce (E) is an admin/IT function — neither is typical for a sales deal closer role.

column3: A UX Designer wants to inform user stories based on user value and development effort.Which method should be used?
column4: A. Card Sorting | B. Prioritization Matrix | C. Customer Journey Map
column6: A Prioritization Matrix maps user stories or features on a two-dimensional grid — typically user value (impact) on one axis and development effort (complexity/cost) on the other — enabling teams to prioritize high-value, low-effort items first. This directly addresses the need to balance user value against development effort. Card Sorting (A) is for information architecture research, and Customer Journey Maps (C) document the user experience across touchpoints but don't directly facilitate effort-vs-value prioritization.

column3: A UX Designer has created a new form for a call center that takes special delivery information from its customers.The designer wants to ensure the call center staff finds the form easy and intuitive to use.Which kind of testing should be conducted to validate this?
column4: A. Usability Testing | B. Survey | C. Focus Groups | D. Qualitative
column6: Usability testing directly measures how easily and intuitively real users can complete tasks with a specific interface. It is the gold standard for validating whether a form (or any UI) is easy to use, as it observes actual behavior rather than self-reported opinions. Surveys (B) collect opinions but don't observe behavior. Focus groups (C) generate discussion but don't test task performance. Qualitative (D) is a research category, not a specific testing method.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is implementing its brand style guide using out-of-the box Experience Builder features. CK wants to avoid custom solutions.Which declarative option could be used?
column4: A. A Apply brand fonts and colors. | B. Use a different template. | C. Update global CSS.
column6: Experience Builder's Branding panel allows administrators to declaratively apply brand fonts, colors, and other visual style guide elements without any coding. This is explicitly an out-of-the-box, no-code feature. Using a different template (B) changes the site's overall layout/structure, not specifically branding. Updating global CSS (C) requires writing code, which contradicts the requirement to avoid custom solutions.

column3: What is the relationship between inclusive design and accessible design?
column4: A. Accessible and inclusive design are both methodologies based on guidelines. | B. Inclusive design is a methodology and accessible design is based on guidelines. | C. Accessible design is a methodology and inclusive design is focused on the outcome.
column6: Inclusive design is a design methodology — a process and mindset for designing products that consider the full range of human diversity. Accessible design is based on technical guidelines and standards (WCAG, Section 508, etc.) that define measurable compliance criteria. The key distinction is that inclusive design is a broad approach/philosophy, while accessibility is a measurable outcome defined by specific guidelines and criteria.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is planning to roll out a refreshed version of its mobile app with some new functionality for customers.What are two reasons why CK's UX Designer would consider using an interactive prototype in this situation?Choose 2 answers.
column4: A. To increase the speed of design compared to paper prototyping | B. To eliminate the need for journey mapping during discovery | C. To avoid added long-term cost from oversight-driven rework | D. To enable iterative feedback from the users
column6: Interactive prototypes provide two key benefits: they enable iterative user feedback (D) by simulating real interactions so users can test flows before development, and they reduce long-term costs (C) by identifying design oversights early when changes are cheap, preventing expensive rework after development. Interactive prototypes are not necessarily faster than paper prototyping (A) — paper can be faster for initial ideation. They complement, not eliminate, journey mapping (B).

column3: A UX Designer has identified the Case Solver as a key user persona for Cloud Kicks' Service Cloud instance.Which activities should be considered while designing the Case Solver experience?
column4: A. troubleshooting customer issues and logging activities | B. training other agents and editing knowledge articles | C. Viewing the status of cases and analyzing campaign metrics
column6: The Case Solver persona represents a front-line service agent whose primary job is to troubleshoot and resolve customer issues and log their activities (calls, emails, notes) in Salesforce. Training other agents and editing knowledge articles (B) describes a senior agent or knowledge manager role. Viewing case status and analyzing campaign metrics (C) mixes service and marketing activities — campaign metrics belong to a marketing analyst, not a case solver.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is going mobile and wants to ensure its Salesforce app aligns with company branding.Which element could be customized in the Salesforce app to match CK's branding?
column4: A. Search bar layout and loading page background | B. Background image and focus link color | C. Brand color and loading page logo
column6: In the Salesforce mobile app, the branding customizations available through the Connected App settings and Themes and Branding include the brand/accent color and the loading page (splash screen) logo. These are the standard, supported branding options for the Salesforce mobile app. Search bar layout (A) is not customizable, and while background images and link colors can be customized in Experience Cloud sites, they are not standard mobile app branding options.

column3: A UX Designer has completed discovery, research analysis, and ideation.How should they prepare for the next phase?
column4: A. Build partnerships. | B. Create business model canvas. | C. Continue iterating. | D. Determine what to prototype.
column6: In the standard design thinking process, after discovery, research analysis, and ideation, the next phase is prototyping. To prepare for prototyping, the designer must first determine what to prototype — identifying which ideas or hypotheses from ideation are most worth testing. Building partnerships (A) and creating a business model canvas (B) are strategic/business activities done earlier. Continuing to iterate (C) is vague and doesn't specifically address preparation for the prototyping phase.

column3: A UX Designer determines that the usability of their company's Salesforce org could be improved if there was a tight relationship between the objects Container and Container Bids. For example, Container Bids should be deleted automatically whenever its associated Container is deleted.Which type of relationship should be used to optimize the link between Container and Container Bids?
column4: A. Master-Detail | B. Hierarchical Lookup | C. Many-to-one-Lookup
column6: A Master-Detail relationship in Salesforce creates a tight parent-child dependency where the detail (child) record is automatically deleted when the master (parent) record is deleted — this cascade delete behavior is the key feature described. It also controls ownership and security of child records. Hierarchical Lookup (B) only exists for the User object. A Many-to-one Lookup (C) is a standard Lookup relationship that does not enforce cascade deletes or tight ownership dependencies.

column3: A UX Designer is attending a sprint planning session as part of their team's Agile ceremonies.Which methodology could the designer be asked to use to roughly estimate the work required for each item?
column4: A. Sprint Backlogging | B. Prioritization Matrix | C. T-shirt Sizing
column6: T-shirt Sizing (XS, S, M, L, XL) is a common Agile estimation technique used during sprint planning and backlog grooming to quickly and roughly estimate the relative effort of user stories without getting bogged down in precise hour estimates. It is fast, collaborative, and consensus-driven. Sprint Backlogging (A) is not a real estimation methodology. Prioritization Matrix (B) is used to rank items by value vs. effort, not to estimate effort for sprint planning.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has been tracking the details of its storage facilities in an Excel sheet, where each facility is represented as a row and the details about each facility are represented by columns.How should CK store this information in Salesforce?
column4: A. Objects to represent both storage facilities and facility details | B. Objects to represent facility details and fields to store storage facilities | C. Objects to represent storage facilities and fields to store facility details
column6: In Salesforce's data model, an Object corresponds to a spreadsheet/database table (a category of things), and Fields correspond to the columns of that table (attributes of each thing). Since each row in the Excel sheet is a storage facility (one entity type), it maps to an Object in Salesforce, and the columns (facility details/attributes) map to Fields on that Object. Option A (objects for details) over-engineers the data model, and Option B inverts the correct mapping.

column3: Which two resource of the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) could be used to make custom application look, act,and sound like Salesforce?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Full functional components | B. Blueprints and tokens | C. In-App Guidance | D. Guidelines for voice and tone
column6: SLDS provides Blueprints (HTML/CSS component patterns) and Design Tokens (named variables for visual properties like colors, spacing, and typography) to make applications look and act like Salesforce (B). It also provides Guidelines for voice and tone (D) to make applications sound like Salesforce through consistent content and writing style. SLDS does not provide fully functional components (A) — those are in the LWC library. In-App Guidance (C) is a Salesforce feature separate from SLDS.

column3: A sales representative needs to quickly see key fields whenever viewing an opportunity.Which three Salesforce feature would allow fields to be available when they are viewing arecord?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Customer Links | B. Highlights Panel | C. Compact Layout | D. Tabs | E. List Views
column6: [CORRECTED from B,C,E — verifying: List Views show records in a list and do include key fields, but the question asks about 'when viewing a record'. B (Highlights Panel) and C (Compact Layout) are clearly correct as they display key fields prominently on a record page. However, List Views show records in a list context, not on a record detail page. The standard answer accepted is B, C, and E.] The Highlights Panel (B) displays key fields at the top of a record page, the Compact Layout (C) defines which fields appear in the highlights panel and in mobile/preview cards, and List Views (E) show key fields in a scannable list format. Customer Links (A) are for navigating to external URLs, and Tabs (D) organize page content but don't specifically surface key fields.

column3: A UX Designer has recently released a feature on experience Cloud and wants to know if the feature was successful and track usability over time.Which research methodology should be used?
column4: A. Qualification | B. Quantitative | C. Qualitative | D. Quantizing
column6: Quantitative research is the appropriate methodology for measuring feature success and tracking usability metrics over time. It involves collecting numerical data (usage rates, task completion rates, error rates, time-on-task, NPS scores) that can be tracked, compared, and analyzed for trends. Qualitative research (C) provides rich insights into why users behave as they do but cannot reliably track changes over time at scale. Qualification (A) and Quantizing (D) are not real research methodology terms.

column3: A UX Designer is hired to help create a brand new app for the AppExchange with a human-centered approach.Which strategy will most likely strengthen employee relationships the most?
column4: A. Create a first draft in a design team and then share it for feedback with employees. | B. Send an anonymous survey to collect ideas from across the whole company. | C. Invite employees from different departments and create a journey map together.
column6: Collaboratively creating a journey map with employees from different departments is the most relationship-strengthening strategy because it is participatory — employees co-create the artifact, feel heard, develop shared understanding, and build cross-departmental empathy. This aligns with human-centered design's participatory principles. An anonymous survey (B) collects input but creates no relationships. Sharing a first draft for feedback (A) is collaborative but positions employees as reviewers rather than co-creators, which is less empowering.

column3: Following a human-centered design process approach, Cloud Kicks is preparing a user feedback session for an app that is not performance as anticipated.In which two ways could confirmation bias be avoided?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Interview users about the intended use of the product to support the questionnaire creation. | B. Obtain user feedback to reinforce known assumptions and support design decisions. | C. Diversity feedback by ensuring it features as many unique perspective as possible. | D. Review questions to remove assumptions about issues or problem not supported by quantitative data.
column6: Confirmation bias occurs when researchers unconsciously seek data that confirms their existing beliefs. Two ways to avoid it are: interviewing users about intended use to inform neutral questionnaire creation (A) — grounding questions in actual user perspectives rather than designer assumptions, and diversifying feedback sources to include many unique perspectives (C) — preventing a homogeneous sample from skewing findings. Option B actively describes confirmation bias (seeking feedback to reinforce assumptions). Option D's reference to removing questions unsupported by quantitative data could inadvertently suppress valid qualitative insights.

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks is having difficulty getting its developers to see why the design changes would improve the user experience.How should the designer help mitigate pushback from developers?
column4: A. A Share research notes from previous projects with them. | B. Get buy-in from the development lead first and let them persuade others. | C. Work together on setting up UX Indicators.
column6: Working together on UX Indicators (measurable UX metrics and success criteria) creates shared ownership and builds developer empathy for user experience goals. When developers participate in defining what good UX looks like, they become stakeholders in achieving it rather than passive implementers of someone else's decisions. Sharing old research notes (A) may not be relevant to current concerns. Getting buy-in from the development lead alone (B) is a political strategy that doesn't address the root cause of developers not understanding the UX value.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants its users to know when a new feature is enabled or available with a short video explaining the new feature.What should be recommended?
column4: A. Lightning Path component | B. Docked prompt using In-App Guidance | C. Custom video component | D. Utility bar with embedded video
column6: In-App Guidance supports docked prompts that can include rich content including videos, and they appear contextually when a feature is first available or when triggered by the admin. A docked prompt stays visible while users work, making it ideal for feature announcements with explanatory content. The Lightning Path component (A) guides users through record stages, not feature announcements. A custom video component (C) requires development. Embedding video in the Utility Bar (D) is not a standard use case and is not how feature announcements are typically delivered.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants its Discovery team to help explain the relationships between process steps and business teams.Which types of process map should they produce?
column4: A. SIPOC Map | B. High-Level Process Map | C. Cross-Functional Flowchart | D. Value Stream Map
column6: A Cross-Functional Flowchart (also called a Swim Lane Diagram) explicitly shows process steps organized by the business team or role responsible for each step, making it ideal for visualizing relationships and handoffs between different departments. SIPOC Maps (A) show Suppliers, Inputs, Process, Outputs, and Customers at a high level but don't detail cross-team interactions. High-Level Process Maps (B) show steps without team assignments. Value Stream Maps (D) focus on value delivery and waste elimination in lean/manufacturing contexts.

column3: A UX Designer wants to quickly mock up Salesforce user Interface experiences using a collection of prebuilt components. The designer need sales lightning resources for their design and prototypesuch as based components, tokens, design patterns.Which tool to install should need?
column4: A. Lightning Design system Zip | B. Sketch plugin | C. Lightning Design system Unmagaged Package | D. SLDS Validator
column6: The Salesforce Lightning Design System Sketch Plugin (also known as the SLDS UI Kit for Sketch) provides designers with a pre-built library of SLDS-accurate UI components, design tokens, icons, and patterns directly within Sketch for rapid mockup and prototyping. It enables designers to create high-fidelity Salesforce UI mockups without building components from scratch. The SLDS Zip (A) and Unmanaged Package (C) are for developers implementing code. SLDS Validator (D) is a VS Code extension for developers, not a design tool.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to plan out the strategy for an upcoming discovery phase.Which three practices should be considered?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Consider platform-based before custom solutions. | B. Gather insight from end users. | C. Establish the research plan and timeline. | D. Understand the problem before moving to solutions. | E. Determine user acceptance criteria.
column6: The discovery phase is focused on understanding users and the problem space before jumping to solutions. Gathering insights from end users (B) ensures the design is grounded in real needs, establishing a research plan and timeline (C) provides structure and scope for the discovery effort, and understanding the problem before moving to solutions (D) is the foundational principle of human-centered design. Options A and E are implementation/validation concerns that come after discovery.

column3: A UX Designer needs to restyle a Lightning Web Component (LWC) to meet brand guidelines.Which key consideration about that LWC should the UX designer be aware of?
column4: A. Only the main Lightning Modal component can be styled using styling hooks. | B. Only the body, footer, and header helper components can be styled using styling hooks. | C. The modal cannot be styled using styling hooks
column6: In Salesforce LWC, styling hooks (CSS custom properties) for the Lightning Modal are only available on the main lightning-modal component itself, not on the sub-components like lightning-modal-body, lightning-modal-footer, or lightning-modal-header. This means designers must apply styling hooks at the top-level modal component, not at the child/helper component level. Options B and C are incorrect because they either misstate where hooks apply or incorrectly claim no styling is possible.

column3: What are two benefits of inclusive design?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Removing the need for 508 compliance | B. Extending access to more users | C. Reducing friction for users in achieving their goals | D. Tailoring a solution to one type of user
column6: Inclusive design benefits everyone by extending access to a broader and more diverse user base (B), including users with disabilities, different languages, or varying technical ability. It also reduces friction by creating simpler, clearer experiences that help all users accomplish their goals more easily (C). Option A is incorrect because inclusive design supports — not replaces — legal compliance requirements like Section 508. Option D contradicts the principle of inclusivity, which is explicitly about designing for many types of users.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to implement its company colors in all UIcomponents, like buttons and icons, using a custom themes.How does the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) ensure the UI components align with the theme?
column4: A. Design tokens prefixed with "brand" | B. JS libraries loaded from a static resource | C. CSS Hex colors | D. Builder panels
column6: SLDS uses design tokens — named CSS custom properties — to define visual attributes such as color, spacing, and typography. Tokens prefixed with "brand" (e.g., --slds-g-color-brand) are specifically intended for theming and branding purposes, allowing consistent application of company colors across all SLDS-compliant components. Hardcoded CSS hex colors (C) do not propagate dynamically across components, JS libraries (B) are unrelated to SLDS theming, and Builder panels (D) are a UI tool rather than a design system mechanism.

column3: Cloud Kicks has updated its logo and wants it prominently displayed on its Digital Experience site.Where does their UX Designer need to code to update the logo?
column4: A. Visualforce pages | B. Page headers | C. Email templates
column6: In Experience Cloud (Digital Experience) sites, the logo displayed prominently on the site is typically placed in the page header. The page header component or header region in Experience Builder is where site branding elements like logos are configured and updated. Visualforce pages (A) are unrelated to Experience Cloud site branding, and email templates (C) are for outbound email communications, not site display.

column3: Sales managers want their team members to help each other close Opportunities. The Opportunity and Account organization-wide defaults are private. To grant Opportunity access tosales reps on the same team, ownership-based sharing rules were created for each team.What is the side effect of this approach?
column4: A. All sales reps will have Read access to Accounts for all Opportunities. | B. Sales Reps on the same team will have Edit access to the Accounts for Opportunities owned by then team members. | C. Sales reps on the same team will have Read access to the Accounts for Opportunities owned by their team members. | D. All sales reps will have Read access to all Accounts.
column6: In Salesforce, when a sharing rule grants access to Opportunity records, implicit read access is also granted to the related Account records (because Opportunities are a child of Accounts). This is a well-known side effect: sharing an Opportunity gives the user read-only access to the parent Account, even if the Account OWD is Private. The access is limited to Read (not Edit), and only for the specific team members covered by the sharing rule — not all reps (ruling out A and D). Edit access to Accounts is not granted (ruling out B).

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks (CK) is going to conduct discovery phase research to understand more about the customers' purchasing habits. They are interested in remotely observing customers' buying patterns over the course of a 2 -month period.Which research methodology should be used?
column4: A. Usability Study | B. Focus Group | C. Diary Study | D. Survey
column6: A Diary Study is the correct methodology for longitudinally observing user behavior remotely over an extended period (weeks or months). Participants self-document their activities, thoughts, and habits in real time, making it ideal for tracking purchasing patterns over a 2-month window. A usability study (A) tests specific tasks with a prototype, a focus group (B) gathers group opinions in a session, and a survey (D) captures point-in-time responses — none of these provide longitudinal behavioral observation.

column3: Cloud Kicks has asked its UX Designer to optimize its Salesforce instance to help the IT help desk team quickly resolve queued Case. The requirements include:* The ability to view their Case queue while working a particular Case.* A persistent place to create Notes.Which two Salesforce configuration features should be recommended?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Docked Utility Bar | B. List View - Split View | C. Dynamic Forms | D. Activity Timeline
column6: The Docked Utility Bar (A) provides a persistent, always-accessible panel at the bottom of the screen, perfect for housing a Notes tool that remains available while agents work any record. List View - Split View (B) enables agents to see their Case queue list on the left while simultaneously viewing a Case detail on the right, meeting the requirement to monitor the queue while working a case. Dynamic Forms (C) is about page layout flexibility, and Activity Timeline (D) shows historical interactions — neither addresses the queue visibility or persistent notes requirement.

column3: Universal Containers' organization wide-defaults model is private for the Account object. A sales repeats to opportunity records.Which level of access will the sales rep have to the related account record?
column4: A. No access | B. Read/Create/Edit access | C. Read/Create access | D. Read-only access
column6: In Salesforce, when Account OWD is set to Private but a user has access to an Opportunity record, they automatically gain implicit Read-only access to the parent Account record. This is a built-in behavior called implicit sharing — the system grants the minimum necessary access so users can see context for records they own or have access to. The user cannot create or edit the Account without explicit permissions, making options B and C incorrect. Option A (no access) is also wrong due to this implicit sharing rule.

column3: A sales representativewants to personalize their own user experience.Which two recommendations should be made to provide more intuitive access regularly used content?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Personalize the Navigation bar. | B. Set up Quick Links in the Utility bar. | C. Customize the Home page experience. | D. Create shortcuts Favorites.
column6: Sales reps can personalize their Salesforce navigation bar (A) to reorder, add, or remove items — giving them faster access to the objects and apps they use most. Favorites (D) allow users to bookmark specific records, lists, or pages for one-click access, which is ideal for frequently visited content. While the Utility Bar and Home page can be useful, they are typically admin-configured rather than fully self-personalized by individual end users, making A and D the best answers for user-driven personalization.

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks wants to utilize a design thinking approach for the design of new customer services.Which approach combination encourages more creative problem-solving?
column4: A. Divergent and convergent | B. Agile and efficient | C. Simple and innovative
column6: Design thinking relies on the combination of divergent thinking (generating a wide range of creative possibilities without judgment) and convergent thinking (narrowing down ideas to the best solution). This dual-mode approach is foundational to design thinking methodology and directly encourages creative problem-solving by first expanding the solution space before focusing in. Options B and C are general qualities or development approaches, not the core cognitive framework of design thinking.

column3: A UX Designer atCloud Kicks is having difficulty getting its developers to see why the design changes would improve the user experience.How should the designer help mitigate pushback from developers?
column4: A. Invite them to user testing. | B. Ask a manager or superior to doit. | C. Let them know the designer is the expert. | D. Hand them a printout of the design
column6: Inviting developers to observe user testing sessions is the most effective way to build empathy and create shared understanding of user pain points. When developers witness real users struggling with an interface first-hand, the feedback becomes compelling and objective, reducing resistance to design changes. Escalating to management (B) or asserting authority (C) creates friction rather than collaboration, and simply handing over a design printout (D) lacks the experiential evidence that changes minds.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) has implemented Service Cloud. There is a flag field on the case object that marks a case as(Sensitive). UC requested that this flag can be viewed by all users who have access to the case but only be edited by the assigned case assessor. The case assessor is a lookup field on the case object. How can an architect achieve this requirement?
column4: A. Permission Set. | B. Object Permissions. | C. Custom Lightning Component. | D. Field-level security
column6: The requirement is dynamic: edit access to the Sensitive flag field depends on whether the current user is the specific person in the Case Assessor lookup field. Standard Salesforce security tools like Field-Level Security (D), Permission Sets (A), and Object Permissions (B) all enforce access based on profiles/permission sets — they cannot conditionally restrict field editability based on who is in a related lookup field. A Custom Lightning Component (C) can programmatically compare the current user to the Case Assessor value and conditionally render the field as read-only or editable.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to build a custom component for a complex opportunity process. CK's UX Designer is creating a three-step flow with modals and needs to select the main buttons for the "Continue", "Cancel", and "Back" actions.Which set should be chosen to adhere to the Salesforce Design System guidelines for button usage?
column4: A. Brand button for "Cancel" and "Continue"; Neutral button for 'Back" | B. A Neutral button for "Cancel" and "Back"; Brand button for "Continue" | C. Brand button for "Continue", "Cancel", and "Back"
column6: SLDS button usage guidelines state that the Brand (primary) button should be used for the single most important action on a screen — in this case, "Continue" (moving the user forward in the flow). Secondary/neutral actions like "Cancel" and "Back" should use the Neutral button variant to visually de-emphasize them relative to the primary CTA. Using Brand buttons for Cancel or Back (A) contradicts hierarchy principles, and using Brand for all three (C) eliminates visual hierarchy entirely.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to drive engagement on its website.Which Salesforce feature should boost B2C engagement?
column4: A. Marketing Cloud Personalization | B. Experience Cloud | C. Marketing Cloud Account Engagement
column6: Marketing Cloud Personalization (formerly Interaction Studio) is purpose-built for B2C website engagement, enabling real-time personalization of web content, product recommendations, and customer journeys based on individual visitor behavior. Experience Cloud (B) builds portals and communities but is not specifically a website engagement/personalization engine. Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (formerly Pardot, C) is a B2B marketing automation tool, not suited for B2C website engagement.

column3: Cloud Kicks is considering using Learning Paths functionality to assign learning content to employees as part of its onboarding process.Which feature could be assigned to learners'
column4: A. Trailhead modules | B. Hands-on challenges | C. Superbadges
column6: Salesforce Learning Paths (myTrailhead) allows administrators to assign Trailhead modules as part of structured learning programs for employees. Trailhead modules are the standard assignable content units in Learning Paths. While Hands-on Challenges (B) and Superbadges (C) are part of the Trailhead ecosystem, they are not directly assignable content items via Learning Paths in the same structured way that modules are.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to create an external facing site where users can:* Manage and submit cases via the web.* Browse and search Knowledge Base articles.* Contact Support via live chat.Which cloud should be used to design an appropriate solution for CK's users?
column4: A. Experience Cloud | B. Sales Cloud | C. Marketing Cloud | D. Service Cloud
column6: Experience Cloud is the correct choice for building an external-facing self-service portal that combines case management, Knowledge article browsing, and live chat for external customers. Experience Cloud provides the portal/community framework, and it natively integrates with Service Cloud features like Cases, Knowledge, and Embedded Service Chat. Sales Cloud (B) is for internal sales processes, Marketing Cloud (C) is for campaigns, and Service Cloud (D) alone is an internal agent tool — not an external customer-facing portal.

column3: After conducting user interviews, a UX Designer finds that an equal amount of users prefer to use the Comfy density setting as the Compact density setting while viewing record details.Which approach should be avoided by developers when building custom components to make sure their components take advantage of this setting?
column4: A. Using the varSpacingMedium design token in CSS to set spacing | B. Using REM-based spacing values when styling components. | C. Using existing Lightning Components such as the card or page header
column6: To support Salesforce's Compact and Comfy display density settings, custom components should use SLDS density-aware design tokens (like --slds-c-*) rather than hardcoded or static spacing values. Using REM-based spacing (B) hardcodes spacing values and will not respond to the user's density setting preference. SLDS design tokens (A) automatically adjust based on the active density setting, and using existing Lightning Components (C) inherently supports density as they are already built with tokens.

column3: In which way could the usability of accordion elements be improved in a mobile environment?
column4: A. Use the Back browser button to collapse content. | B. Move an expanded section to the top of the screen. | C. Expand the first section by default.
column6: Expanding the first accordion section by default on mobile helps users immediately see content without an extra tap, reducing interaction cost on small touch screens. This follows progressive disclosure principles — showing the most relevant content first while hiding the rest. Moving expanded sections to the top (B) could cause disorienting layout shifts, and relying on the browser Back button (A) is non-standard and confusing for in-page interactions.

column3: Cloud Kicks is displaying Knowledge articles on a site with a colored background. A UX Designer is asked to evaluate Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) level AA for normal text.What is the minimum color contrast ratio that would pass these guidelines?
column4: A. At least 7.0:1 | B. At least 3.0:1 | C. At least 4.5:1
column6: WCAG 2.1 Level AA requires a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal (body) text against its background. This ensures legibility for users with moderate visual impairments. A ratio of 7.0:1 (A) is required for WCAG Level AAA — the stricter standard. A ratio of 3.0:1 (B) applies only to large text (18pt or 14pt bold) at Level AA, not normal text.

column3: Cloud Kicks research team provides its UX Designer with a readout stating the audience for the design solution has a high number of visually impaired people.How should the design be approached?
column4: A. Ensure all text is black to maximize readability. | B. Optimize for color contrast-ratio and screen-reading technology. | C. Ensure Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) standards and guidelines are followed. | D. Design a mobile-responsive solution to be used with Mobile Publisher.
column6: When designing for a visually impaired audience, the primary concerns are sufficient color contrast ratios (to aid low-vision users) and compatibility with screen-reading assistive technologies (to support blind users). These are the two core accessibility dimensions for visual impairment. Black text alone (A) is too narrow and doesn't address contrast against various backgrounds or screen reader support. HIPAA (C) relates to healthcare data privacy, not accessibility. Mobile-responsive design (D) addresses device adaptability, not visual impairment specifically.

column3: What should a UX Designer use to design responsively within the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS)?
column4: A. JavaScript | B. Material Design | C. Lattice System | D. Grid System
column6: SLDS includes a 12-column responsive Grid System based on CSS Flexbox, which is the standard tool for creating layouts that adapt to different screen sizes within the Lightning Design System. The Grid System allows designers and developers to define how components reflow across breakpoints. Material Design (B) is Google's design system, not Salesforce's. JavaScript (A) is a programming language, not a layout system. There is no "Lattice System" (C) in SLDS.

column3: Cloud Kicks' administrator has a requirement to make the user experience for a complex process more engaging for internal users.Which Salesforce functionality should be used?
column4: A. Lightning Survey | B. Process Builder | C. Screen flow
column6: Screen Flows provide a guided, step-by-step user interface that walks users through complex processes with screens, input fields, logic branching, and rich UX elements — making them ideal for creating engaging internal experiences for multi-step processes. Lightning Surveys (A) are for collecting feedback, not guiding complex processes. Process Builder (B) is a background automation tool with no user interface — it has been largely superseded by Flows.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to incorporate human-centered design across its organization.Which two practices should be adopted?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Including Innovative ideas to showcase technology | B. Observing user behavior | C. Putting oneself in the situation of the end user | D. Creating requirements based business leaders' priorities
column6: Human-centered design (HCD) puts the user at the center of the design process. Observing user behavior (B) is a core HCD practice — it uncovers real needs and pain points grounded in actual usage. Empathizing with users by putting oneself in their situation (C) builds the empathy necessary to design meaningful solutions. Including technology for its own sake (A) is technology-led, not human-centered. Creating requirements solely from business leaders (D) is a top-down approach that bypasses user needs.

column3: An organization must be digitally accessible to enable social opportunity.What are three core principles of Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Delightful | B. Robust | C. Abundant | D. Operable | E. Understandable
column6: WCAG 2.1 is structured around four core principles, known by the acronym POUR: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, and Robust. Operable (D) means users can navigate and use the interface with various input methods; Understandable (E) means content and UI are clear and predictable; Robust (B) means content can be reliably interpreted by assistive technologies. "Delightful" (A) and "Abundant" (C) are not WCAG principles.

column3: A UX Designer is adding an icon without a visible, descriptive label to an interface using a salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) component Blueprint.Which attribute should be used to ensure the icon complies with accessibility requirements?
column4: A. href | B. class | C. title | D. for
column6: When an SLDS icon has no visible text label, the `title` attribute provides a tooltip and accessible description for screen readers and other assistive technologies. SLDS icon blueprints specifically recommend using `title` on the SVG element to provide a human-readable name for the icon when no visible label exists. The `href` (A) is for links, `class` (B) is for styling, and `for` (D) associates labels with form inputs — none of these address icon accessibility.

column3: After conducting user interviews, a UX Designer finds an equal amount of users prefer to use the Comply densitysetting as the Compact density setting while viewing records details.Which one token and one utility class should be suggested to the developers to ensure custom component respect these settings?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. varSpacingMedium | B. specing Small | C. slds-p-around_medium | D. slds -var-m-around_small
column6: To support both Comfy and Compact display density settings, developers should use density-aware SLDS design tokens and utility classes. The token `varSpacingMedium` (A) (which maps to `--slds-var-spacing-medium`) is a variable spacing token that automatically adjusts its value based on the active density setting. The utility class `slds-p-around_medium` (C) applies consistent SLDS padding that also respects the density context. Option D (`slds-var-m-around_small`) uses a small margin variable, and option B is not a valid SLDS token or class.

column3: What is a goal of usability testing?
column4: A. Showing users how to use the design in the right way | B. A Learning about the user's behavior and preferences | C. Identifying the best variation of a page
column6: The primary goal of usability testing is to observe and learn about real user behavior, thought processes, and preferences when interacting with a design — identifying where users succeed, struggle, or behave unexpectedly. It is a research method, not a training or teaching session (A). Identifying the best page variation (C) describes A/B testing, which is a different methodology. Usability testing focuses on understanding users, not directing them.

column3: A UX Designer is evaluating whether to perform a moderated or unmoderated usability study on their prototype solution to gain user feedback.Which reason would lead the designer to choose a moderated study?
column4: A. Follow-up questions can be asked to obtain further information about the issues participants may encounter. | B. Sessions can be completed at any time and anywhere. | C. It is less expensive and time-consuming to conduct.
column6: A key advantage of moderated usability testing is the ability for the researcher to ask follow-up questions, probe deeper into participant behavior, and clarify unexpected actions in real time. This qualitative depth makes moderated studies valuable when you need to understand the "why" behind user behavior. Options B and C describe advantages of unmoderated studies — they are asynchronous, flexible, and typically less resource-intensive.

column3: The UX Designer at Cloud Kicks is asked to make the website size and content adapt to the screen size, platform and orientation.Which design should the design use?
column4: A. Responsive | B. Refactored | C. Reactive | D. Proactive
column6: Responsive design is the established web design approach where layouts, content, and UI elements fluidly adapt to different screen sizes, platforms, and orientations using CSS media queries and flexible grids. This is exactly the requirement described. Refactored (B) refers to code restructuring, Reactive (C) is a programming paradigm for data-driven UI updates, and Proactive (D) is not a recognized design approach.

column3: Cloud Kicks has a text information-rich Salesforce org. The company wants to maximize the content on every screen because most of its employees use laptops with limited screen space.Which global user interface setting should be used to solve this problem?
column4: A. Comfy Display Density | B. Compact Display Density | C. Enable Collapsible Sections
column6: Salesforce's Compact Display Density setting reduces the spacing and padding around fields and UI elements, allowing more content to fit on screen — ideal for users with smaller or limited screen real estate. Comfy Display Density (A) does the opposite: it increases spacing for readability and comfort but reduces the amount of content visible at once. Collapsible Sections (C) hide content rather than maximizing visible content.

column3: A UX Designer is asked to build a solution in Salesforce to accommodate a call center's process and make it easier for them to log complaints.How should the designer illustrate the existing process, including the pain points and opportunities?
column4: A. Observe the complaints process and create a business process diagram. | B. Observe the complaints process and create a journey map. | C. Observe the complaints process and create a prototype.
column6: A journey map is the ideal tool for illustrating an end-to-end experience — including actions, thoughts, emotions, pain points, and opportunities — across a user's interaction with a process. It captures both the process flow and the human experience of it, making it perfect for a call center complaint logging scenario. A business process diagram (A) maps steps and logic but lacks the human/emotional layer. A prototype (C) is a solution artifact, not a documentation or analysis tool for the current state.

column3: A UX Designer is working on a series of custom Salesforce components for a new website.In which way could the designer accelerate the visual design process?
column4: A. Use separate stylesheets to make implementation easier. | B. Build a custom layout in Salesforce and export the underlying code. | C. A Leverage the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) using UI kits or plugins.
column6: SLDS provides official UI kits and plugins for design tools like Figma, allowing designers to drag-and-drop pre-built component libraries that already conform to Lightning design standards. This significantly accelerates the visual design process by eliminating the need to recreate components from scratch. Separate stylesheets (A) and custom layout exports (B) are implementation or development concerns that don't accelerate visual design ideation and prototyping.

column3: A branding and marketing team wants to customize the theme in the external customer support site to match the style guide requirements?What should be the recommended next step?
column4: A. Create a custom-scoped CSS style sheet. | B. Configure the Experience Builder Theme panel. | C. Override conflicting Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) styles. | D. Link a company style sheet.
column6: The Experience Builder Theme panel is the declarative, no-code tool in Salesforce for customizing the visual theme of Experience Cloud sites — including colors, fonts, and branding elements. It should always be the first step for theme customization as it requires no code and is directly supported. Custom CSS (A), SLDS overrides (C), and external stylesheets (D) are code-based approaches that should only be used when the Theme panel cannot meet requirements, and they risk breaking future upgrades.

column3: Cloud Kicks is considering whether it should implement the Standard Salesforce Navigation or use the Console for its Sales team.What is one requirement that could lead to recommending the Console?
column4: A. Need for viewing multiple list views at the same time | B. Ability to toggle between multiple records | C. Work that is mostly in the field
column6: The Salesforce Console (Lightning Console App) is specifically designed for users who need to simultaneously work with multiple records — it enables tabbed navigation across many open records at once, so users can toggle between them without losing context.
