This makes it ideal for high-volume workers like service agents or sales reps managing many deals. Viewing multiple list views (A) can be done in standard navigation with split view. Field work (C) would point to mobile solutions rather than console.

column3: A UX Designer wants to plan and communicate the intended page layouts of a community portal.Which tool should they use?
column4: A. Journey Mapping | B. Wireframes | C. Personas | D. Process Flows
column6: Wireframes are low-to-mid fidelity representations of page layouts, showing the structure, content hierarchy, and placement of UI elements. They are the standard tool for planning and communicating intended page layouts to stakeholders and developers. Journey maps (A) illustrate user experiences over time, personas (C) describe user types, and process flows (D) document step-by-step workflows — none of these communicate page layouts.

column3: Cloud Kicks is incorporating Relationship Design principles into its business model and customer offerings wherever possible.Which principle would represent their company strategy?
column4: A. Reframing products in terms of outputs over outcomes | B. Prioritizing engagement over number of impressions | C. Highlighting product benefits over uncovering customer needs
column6: Relationship Design focuses on building meaningful, long-term connections with customers rather than transactional or superficial interactions. Prioritizing engagement over impressions (B) reflects a depth-over-breadth philosophy — valuing quality interactions and ongoing relationships over raw reach metrics. Option A (outputs over outcomes) and Option C (benefits over needs) both represent product-centric thinking that contradicts the customer-first ethos of Relationship Design.

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks has the requirements and some user scenarios but wants to test how a new feature will be received by the user.What should the designer create and show to the user to test the content and structure of the new feature?
column4: A. Wireframe Prototype | B. Heuristic Review | C. Diary Study | D. Task Analysis
column6: A wireframe prototype is the right artifact for testing content structure and layout with users early in the design process. It combines the structural clarity of wireframes with enough interactivity to simulate user flows, making it ideal for gathering feedback on information architecture and feature design before investing in high-fidelity production. A heuristic review (B) is an expert evaluation, a diary study (C) tracks behavior over time, and a task analysis (D) documents existing task breakdowns — none involve showing a new design to users for feedback.

column3: A group of sales users needs to be guided step by step through a new process using Floating or Docked Prompts.Their administrative teamwants to the ability to see the adoption of this assistance via Reports and Dashboards.
column4: A. in-App Guidance Prompts using myTrailhead | B. Custom build using Salesforce Flow | C. Basic In-App Guidance Prompts | D. Use the Walkthrough App from AppExchange
column6: [CORRECTED from C to D] While Basic In-App Guidance Prompts support Floating and Docked formats, they do not natively provide reporting and dashboard analytics on prompt adoption. The Walkthrough App from AppExchange (such as WalkMe or similar tools integrated via AppExchange) provides step-by-step guided walkthroughs with usage tracking and analytics that can be surfaced in reports and dashboards. This combination of guided walkthroughs plus adoption analytics is best served by an AppExchange solution. Note: some exam versions accept C if the question focuses purely on the prompt type — verify with your study materials.

column3: A UX Designer wants touse Paths to provide guidance about which activities sales representatives should be doing at each stage of the opportunity lifecycle.Which two elements could be used in the Path's Guidance for Success sections?
column4: A. Lightning Component | B. Images and Links | C. Progress Indicator | D. Rich Text
column6: Salesforce Path's "Guidance for Success" section supports Rich Text (D), which allows formatted instructions, bullet points, and emphasis, as well as Images and Links (B) to provide visual aids and external references for each stage. Lightning Components (A) cannot be embedded directly in the Guidance for Success section. Progress Indicators (C) are an element of the Path component itself (the stage bar) but are not configurable content within the Guidance for Success area.

column3: A UX Designer is designing a Service Cloud implementation for service representatives who should be able to view the history of cases that a specific customer has submitted.Which hierarchy of information should the representative go through to view that list?
column4: A. All Contacts > Contact Detail > Account Detail > Case Related List | B. All Contacts > Contact Detail > Case Related List > Account Detail | C. All Cases > Case Detail > Contact Detail > Account Related List | D. All Accounts > Account Detail > Contact Detail > Case Related List
column6: In Salesforce's standard data model, Cases are related to Contacts, and Contacts are related to Accounts. To view all cases a specific customer (Account) has submitted, the logical navigation hierarchy is: start at All Accounts, open the Account Detail, navigate to the related Contact, and then view the Case Related List on the Contact or Account. Option D correctly represents the Account-centric hierarchy that provides the most complete customer case history view. Options A and B start from Contacts which doesn't give the full account-level picture.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has made a commitment to incorporating human-centered design and is now collaborating with its Sales team to redesign some of its key sales processes in Salesforce.Which conceptual approach would describe CK's new design solutions?
column4: A. V2MOM | B. Agile | C. Desirable, Feasible, Viable
column6: The "Desirable, Feasible, Viable" framework is the conceptual core of human-centered design, originating from IDEO/design thinking. Desirable means the solution meets real human needs, Feasible means it can be technically built, and Viable means it makes business sense. This intersection defines great HCD solutions. V2MOM (A) is Salesforce's internal goal-alignment framework, and Agile (B) is a project/development methodology — neither describes the human-centered design lens applied to solution quality.

column3: A UX Designer is creating a customer support site in Experience Builder that will be internationalized across12 different countries.Which two design considerations should be made when planning for this site?
column4: A. Countries may read text in a different (right to left vs. left to right) and layoutswill be to be adjusted. | B. Country flags used as links to adjust languages provide an ideal way to switch between locales or languages for users. | C. Colors may have different contrast ratios in some countries and need adjust contrast for proper visibilityby users. | D. Colors may have different culture meanings in different countries, changing the intent of UI elements.
column6: When internationalizing a site, two critical design considerations are text directionality (A) — RTL languages like Arabic and Hebrew require mirrored layouts — and cultural color meanings (D) — colors like red or white can have very different associations across cultures, impacting how UI intent is perceived. Using country flags as language selectors (B) is actually a UX anti-pattern because flags represent countries, not languages (e.g., Spanish is spoken in many countries). Color contrast ratios (C) are a universal accessibility standard, not country-specific.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has identified that its individual agents send out messaging and communication that is often not aligned with the company's overall branding and communication style.Which Salesforce solutions should be implemented to help CK deliver consistently branded content at every touchpoint?
column4: A. A Distributed Marketing and Journey Builder | B. Email Builder and Customer 360 | C. Service Cloud and Letterhead
column6: Distributed Marketing (part of Marketing Cloud) enables centralized marketing teams to create pre-approved, brand-compliant content that individual agents or sales reps can personalize and send — ensuring consistent branding across all touchpoints. Journey Builder orchestrates the multi-channel customer journey with that branded content. This combination directly solves the problem of rogue, inconsistent agent communications. Email Builder (B) and Letterhead (C) are narrower tools that don't enforce brand governance at the agent communication level.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to drive engagement on its website.Which two Salesforce features should boost engagement?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Einstein Bots | B. Automated Invitations | C. MyTrailhead | D. Salesforce Connect
column6: Einstein Bots (A) drive website engagement by providing intelligent, conversational chat experiences that proactively assist and engage visitors in real time. Automated Invitations (B) — part of Embedded Service / Live Agent — proactively trigger chat invitations based on visitor behavior (e.g., time on page, page type), directly boosting engagement. MyTrailhead (C) is an internal learning platform for employees. Salesforce Connect (D) is a data integration tool for external objects, not a user engagement feature.

column3: What would it mean for the user when designing perceivable content?
column4: A. The content should only be presented in a visual format. | B. The content should only be presented in an audio format. | C. The content should be presented in a way that can be convertible between different formats.
column6: "Perceivable" is one of the four WCAG principles and means that all information and UI components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive — including converting between modalities (e.g., text to audio via screen readers, captions for video). Designing perceivable content means it is not tied to a single sensory channel. Options A and B are both wrong because they restrict content to a single format, which would exclude users who cannot use that modality.

column3: A UX Designer is going to create a custom app for a new team of service agents.Which three parts of the user interface could be customized?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Tabs within the apps's navigation bar | B. Relationship between standard objects | C. Page layouts of the records | D. Details to be shown in the records highlights panels | E. Opportunity lead scoring
column6: When building a custom Salesforce app, a UX Designer can customize: the navigation bar tabs (A) to show only relevant objects and items, page layouts (C) to control which fields and components appear on records, and the Highlights Panel (D) to surface the most important record details prominently. Relationships between standard objects (B) are fixed in the Salesforce data model and cannot be changed through UI customization. Opportunity lead scoring (E) is an AI/analytics feature, not a UI customization.

column3: Cloud Kicks' development team is working on the build of a new custom component using VS Code. They often have new CSS classes and properties conflicting with the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS), What should simplify working with SLDS in Lightning Components?
column4: A. Install SLDS creator from AppExchange | B. Install SLDS Validator extension for VS Code | C. Install Lightning Design System Plugin for Sketch
column6: The SLDS Validator is a VS Code extension that provides real-time linting, autocomplete, and validation of SLDS classes and tokens directly in the code editor. It helps developers identify invalid or conflicting SLDS class names as they type, preventing CSS conflicts before they occur. There is no "SLDS creator" in AppExchange (A), and the Lightning Design System Plugin for Sketch (C) is a design tool for Sketch, not a development aid for VS Code.

column3: Which elements of visual design should be used to better translate style and branding guidelines?
column4: A. Typography, Color, Imagery | B. Wireframes, Personas, Blueprints | C. Sketching, Wireframes, Storyboards
column6: Typography, Color, and Imagery are the three foundational visual design elements used to express and communicate a brand's style guidelines. Typography defines voice and readability, color establishes brand identity and emotion, and imagery conveys the brand's visual tone. Options B and C are design process artifacts (wireframes, personas, storyboards) used for planning and communication — they describe structure and process, not visual brand expression.

column3: A UX Designer is using the human-centered design approach to redesign a portal that medical staff use to report on patient demographics.Which activity should come first in the process?
column4: A. Observing the medical staffwhile they use their existing portal | B. Estimating the cost to complete development of the portal | C. Writing technical requirements for how the portal should function | D. Designing a mockup of how the new portal will look
column6: Human-centered design always begins with the Empathize phase — deeply understanding users in their context. Observing medical staff using their existing portal reveals real workflows, pain points, and unmet needs before any solution design begins. Starting with cost estimation (B), technical requirements (C), or mockups (D) skips the empathy phase and risks designing solutions based on assumptions rather than observed user reality.

column3: A UX Designer wants to explore sample code for Lightning components and see how changing to code affects the visual appearance in real time, without deploying any code to a Salesforce org.Whichtwo resources should help accomplish this?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Lightning Design System Website | B. Local Development Server | C. Lightning Web Component Recipes App | D. Develop Center's Lightning Component Library
column6: The SLDS Website (A) includes interactive component blueprints with live code examples that can be modified and previewed directly in the browser without deploying to an org — perfect for exploring visual changes. The Local Development Server (B) (part of Salesforce CLI) allows developers to preview and iterate on LWC components locally with real-time hot-reload, also without requiring org deployment. The LWC Recipes App (C) requires deployment to an org, and the Developer Center's Component Library (D) is a reference document without live editing capability.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has finished conducting research and has synthesized the findings. CK now plans to collaboratively redesign services with stakeholders to address issues uncovered in the study.Which process should be used?
column4: A. Service-Blueprinting Workshop | B. Management Committee Meeting | C. Pitch Session | D. Service Usability Testing
column6: A Service Blueprinting Workshop is a collaborative design process where stakeholders co-create a visual map of the end-to-end service, including frontstage interactions, backstage processes, and support systems. It is specifically designed for collaboratively redesigning services based on research findings, making it the ideal next step after research synthesis. A management committee meeting (B) is decision-making, a pitch session (C) is presenting, and usability testing (D) is user evaluation — none are collaborative service redesign processes.

column3: Cloud Kicks has an existing customer Experience Cloud portal that is performing well.Which has the highest probability of increasing customer engagement?
column4: A. Choosing personalized branding | B. Customizing page layouts | C. Recognizing peers with badges
column6: Gamification elements like peer recognition with badges significantly boost community engagement by creating social incentives — users return to earn recognition, acknowledge others, and build status. This taps into intrinsic motivators like belonging and achievement. Personalized branding (A) improves aesthetics but doesn't drive behavioral engagement. Customizing page layouts (B) improves usability but doesn't motivate ongoing participation. Badges and recognition are proven drivers of community activity and return visits.

column3: A UX Designer needs to restyle a Lightning Experience model to meet brand guidelines.Which two steps should the designer take?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Use design tokens. | B. Configure properties in App Builder. | C. Find the appropriate component blueprint. | D. Use a Lightning flow.
column6: To restyle a Lightning component to meet brand guidelines, a UX designer should first find the appropriate SLDS component blueprint (C) to understand the component's structure, available styling hooks, and correct CSS class names. Then, design tokens (A) should be applied to map brand colors, spacing, and typography to the component in a maintainable, system-aware way. App Builder (B) is for configuring page layouts declaratively, and Lightning Flows (D) are for process automation — neither is a restyling approach.

column3: Cloud Kicks has identified a Trusted Advisor persona as their primary focus for the next iteration.Which key business process defining their work should be analyzed to make sure the delivered experience fits their needs?
column4: A. Maintaining relationships with existing customers | B. Managing sales pipeline and forecasting | C. Customizing and administering Salesforce
column6: A "Trusted Advisor" persona is characterized by deep, ongoing relationships with clients — their primary value comes from nurturing and maintaining those relationships over time, not from transactional sales pipeline management. Analyzing the relationship maintenance process (A) reveals the workflows, touchpoints, and information needs that the experience must support. Pipeline management (B) is more appropriate for a Sales Rep persona, and Salesforce administration (C) relates to an Admin persona.

column3: A UX Designer presents a creative design approach to solving end-user problems, beginning with identifying their needs and ending with creating solutions that meet those needs.Which approach is being followed?
column4: A. Salesforce Declarative Design | B. Digital Declarative Design | C. Human-Centered Design | D. User Interface Design
column6: Human-Centered Design (HCD) is precisely the approach that starts with deeply understanding and identifying end-user needs (empathize and define phases) and iterates through to creating and testing solutions that meet those needs. The end-to-end arc from needs identification to solution creation is the defining characteristic of HCD. Declarative Design (A, B) refers to configuration-based development in Salesforce, and User Interface Design (D) is focused only on visual/interaction design, not the full needs-to-solution process.

column3: A Sales team is having trouble interacting with Contact records in Sales Cloud. They cannot find the information they need, and many of the visible record fields are not relevant to sales activities. On top of that, they complain the page load time is very long.Which approach should be recommended to improve their experience?
column4: A. Run the Salesforce Optimizer App to identify fields not in use. | B. Move all unused fields to a separate section at the bottom of the page. | C. Implement In-App Guidance to help users focus on the most important fields.
column6: The Salesforce Optimizer App analyzes the org and identifies unused fields, redundant configurations, and performance issues — providing actionable recommendations to clean up the data model. Removing or archiving genuinely unused fields addresses both the clutter problem (irrelevant fields) and the page load performance issue (fewer fields = faster load). Moving unused fields to the bottom (B) still loads them and doesn't solve performance. In-App Guidance (C) helps users navigate existing complexity but doesn't fix the root cause of field overload.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to determine whether or not the Experience Cloud site it is launching is intuitive.CK's UX Designer is going to conduct a usability study.What should be one of the first steps when planning this study?
column4: A. Define the goals of the study. | B. Design changes to the site. | C. Design the information architecture. | D. Analyze the results of the study.
column6: Defining the goals of the study is always the first step in planning any usability study because it establishes what questions you are trying to answer and what success looks like. Without clear goals, it is impossible to design appropriate tasks, recruit the right participants, or know which metrics to collect. Designing changes (B) or analyzing results (D) come after the study, and information architecture (C) is a design activity, not a research planning step.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has already identified its user personas. The UX Designer wants to synthesize what CK knows about the users so that a shared understanding is created with the rest of the organization.Which tool should be used to share this insight?
column4: A. A/BTesting | B. Full Recorded Interviews | C. Empathy Map | D. Heuristic Review
column6: An Empathy Map is specifically designed to synthesize and communicate what a team knows about users — capturing what users say, think, feel, and do — in a format that builds shared understanding across an organization. A/B Testing (A) is a quantitative comparison method, full recorded interviews (B) are raw data not a synthesis tool, and a heuristic review (D) evaluates interface usability rather than communicating user insights.

column3: The UX Designer at Cloud Kicks is considering using a custom Lightning component to fulfill a specific business requirement.Which two best practices should be considered?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Prioritize Aura markup even if there is a Lightning Web Component (LWC) available. | B. Find the closest Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) Blueprint to help inform the custom Lightning component. | C. Create HTML markup and link the SLDS stylesheet via static Resource. | D. Exhaust the list of available base Lightning component in the component Library.
column6: Before building a custom component, a best practice is to first exhaust all available base Lightning components in the Component Library (D) to avoid reinventing existing functionality. When a custom component is truly necessary, referencing the closest SLDS Blueprint (B) ensures the component aligns with Salesforce design standards and maintains visual consistency. Prioritizing Aura over LWC (A) is incorrect since LWC is the modern standard, and linking SLDS via static resource (C) is an outdated approach that can cause version conflicts.

column3: A UX Designer needs to create a visual representation of a user's series of steps to achieve a meaningful goal.Which UX design method should be used?
column4: A. User Persona | B. Site Map | C. Lightning Flow | D. User Flow
column6: A User Flow is a diagram that maps out the sequential steps a user takes to accomplish a specific goal, showing decision points and paths through a system. A User Persona (A) describes who the user is, not what steps they take. A Site Map (B) shows information architecture and navigation structure. Lightning Flow (C) is a Salesforce automation tool, not a UX design method.

column3: Cloud Kicks has already identified its user personas and is working with a UX Designer who wants to synthesize what the company knows about its users to create a shared understanding with the rest of the organization.Which tool should the designer use?
column4: A. Executive Summary | B. Empathy Map | C. Prioritize Backlog
column6: An Empathy Map synthesizes research findings about users into a visual, shareable format that helps entire teams — including non-designers — develop a common understanding of user needs, behaviors, and feelings. An executive summary (A) is a business document, not a user insight tool. Prioritizing a backlog (C) is a project management activity, not a method for building shared user understanding.

column3: Cloud Kicks has identified that its users are having difficulty figuring out where to look on a web page due to the number of design elements.Which three considerations should be made to improve the visual hierarchy of the page?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Grouping proximity and common regions | B. Scale using relative size to signal importance | C. Continuity keep all typeface the same size | D. Proportion, keep allelement a similar size | E. Color and contract, saturation between the elements
column6: Visual hierarchy is improved by using grouping and proximity (A) to cluster related elements, scale/relative size (B) to signal which elements are most important, and color and contrast (E) to draw attention and differentiate elements. Keeping all typefaces the same size (C) and all elements a similar size (D) would actually reduce visual hierarchy by making everything appear equally important, making it harder for users to prioritize where to look.

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks has been asked to gather insights for a newly released feature at scale from a Specific set of users in the flow of their work.Which approach should be used?
column4: A. In-App Feedback | B. User Interviews | C. A/B Test | D. Survey
column6: In-App Feedback allows organizations to collect insights from a specific set of users at scale while they are actively using the application — meaning feedback is gathered in context, in the flow of work. User interviews (B) are qualitative and not scalable to hundreds of users at once. A/B Testing (C) compares two design variants rather than gathering open-ended feature insights. Surveys (D) are typically sent outside of the product flow, not contextual in the moment of use.

column3: The Service team at Cloud Kicks has complained about having too many list views available, making it hard to find the relevant ones.In which way could their experience be most effectively improved?
column4: A. Remove low list view from public view. | B. Update Sharing Rules so users only see records they need to see. | C. Allow users to manage public list views
column6: Removing low-usage list views from public view directly addresses the root problem of too many list views cluttering the interface, making it easier for users to find relevant ones. Updating sharing rules (B) controls which records users can see, not which list views are available. Allowing users to manage public list views (C) could lead to further inconsistency and is not a top-down solution to declutter the experience for the whole team.

column3: A UX Designer is creating a site for delivery within Builder for a customer who has strict requirements is stay focusedon out-of-the-box styling and components only.Which three methods would deliver a branded experience?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Select a footer and configure which social media links to display. | B. Display custom variations of pages based on user behavior. | C. Select a theme and customize content including copy and imagery. | D. Use the theme editor to adjust fonts, text case, colors, and site logo. | E. Create flexible layouts for pages with unique background images.
column6: Within Experience Builder's out-of-the-box capabilities, you can configure the footer and social media links (A), select a theme and update content like copy and imagery (C), and use the Theme Editor to adjust fonts, text case, colors, and the site logo (D) — all without custom code. Displaying page variations based on user behavior (B) requires additional configuration beyond simple branding. Creating flexible layouts with unique background images (E) may require custom development beyond OOTB styling.

column3: Cloud Kicks has requested feature enhancements as a result of user acceptance testing.In which three ways could the changes be effectively analyzed and implemented?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Determine the change in scope and impact of each enhancement request. | B. Develop and deploy all enhancement requests before going live. | C. Reduce unexpected results by configuring and testing in & sandbox. | D. Deploy enhancements with the feature and adjust scope accordingly. | E. Create a prioritization list and perform a feasibility analysis.
column6: When enhancement requests arise from UAT, best practice is to analyze scope and impact (A) to understand effort and risk, create a prioritization list with feasibility analysis (E) to determine what is realistic to implement, and configure and test changes in a sandbox (C) to reduce unexpected results in production. Deploying all enhancements before going live (B) is impractical and risky. Deploying enhancements alongside the original feature without proper analysis (D) can introduce scope creep.

column3: A UX Designer has completed usability testing on a newly designed case management system and has gathered many observations from the test participants.What should the UX Designer consider while presenting the findings?
column4: A. Be specific about the issues testers encountered. | B. Report only the new issues, and omit already known bugs. | C. Include the names of participants in the report.
column6: Being specific about the issues testers encountered ensures that the development and design teams have actionable, clear information needed to prioritize and address problems effectively. Omitting already known bugs (B) provides an incomplete picture and may misrepresent the severity of the overall experience. Including participant names (C) violates research ethics and participant confidentiality, which is a standard practice to protect.

column3: Which two steps should a UX Designer take to create and deliver responsible and transparent AI technology? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Collect as much data from the user as possible for a catered experience. | B. Design AI that is seamless enough so the user does not notice. | C. Document model cards to clarify intended context and use cases. | D. Provide clear explanations of AI predictions or recommendations.
column6: Responsible and transparent AI design requires documenting model cards (C) that describe the AI model's intended use cases, limitations, and context — enabling accountability and informed use. Providing clear explanations of AI predictions or recommendations (D) ensures users understand why the AI is suggesting something, building trust and enabling informed decision-making. Collecting maximum user data (A) raises privacy concerns and is not inherently responsible. Making AI invisible (B) is the opposite of transparency.

column3: A UX Designer is creating an experience to help organize content into collapsible sections.Which Lightning component should be used?
column4: A. Einstein Next Best Action | B. Lightning Toggle | C. Accordion | D. Highlights Panel
column6: The Accordion component in Salesforce Lightning is specifically designed to organize content into collapsible sections, allowing users to expand or collapse each section to manage information density. Einstein Next Best Action (A) surfaces AI-driven recommendations, Lightning Toggle (B) is a binary on/off switch, and the Highlights Panel (D) displays key record fields at the top of a record page — none of these collapse content into sections.

column3: During discovery, a UX Designer finds that most sellers like the idea of using the utility bar for easy access and productivity.Which three standard use cases could be recommended for using the utility bar to enhance the user experience? Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Quick access to Lightning Dialer | B. Perform common tasks without navigating away from page | C. Access to view a list of records favorited for quick access | D. Access to a consistent custom help menu across the org for all users | E. Access at any time to a customized set of items specific to an app
column6: The Salesforce utility bar is a persistent footer that enables users to perform common tasks without leaving their current page (B), access their favorited records list (C), and access a customized set of items specific to the app they are working in (E). Quick access to Lightning Dialer (A) is a valid utility bar use case in practice, but it is more specific than the standard UX recommendations. A consistent help menu (D) is typically handled via the Help Menu feature in Salesforce Setup, not the utility bar.

column3: A UX Designer needs to declutter the Highlights panel for a custom object's Lightning page. The team that uses this object explained there are too many action buttons; only specific actions are used for each status of the record.Which Lightning Record Page feature should be used to solve this problem?
column4: A. Audiences | B. Dynamic Forms | C. A Dynamic Actions
column6: Dynamic Actions allows you to control which action buttons appear in the Highlights Panel based on filter criteria such as record field values (e.g., Status). This means users only see the relevant actions for the current state of the record, directly solving the problem of too many buttons cluttering the panel. Audiences (A) control component visibility based on user attributes, not record field values. Dynamic Forms (B) control field and section visibility on the record page layout, not action button visibility.

column3: In which two ways could the usability of accordion elements be improved in a mobile environment?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Include persistent headings. | B. Nest an accordion inside of another | C. Use the ''back'' browser button to collapse content | D. Only allow users to open one selection at a time.
column6: On mobile devices, persistent headings (A) ensure users can always see the section labels even when scrolling within expanded content, maintaining orientation. Allowing only one section to be open at a time (D) reduces the amount of content displayed simultaneously, which is critical for small screens where space is limited. Nesting accordions (B) adds complexity and depth that is particularly problematic on mobile. Relying on the browser back button (C) to collapse content is a non-standard interaction that would confuse users.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to incorporates human-centered design across its organization Which two practices should beadopted
column4: A. Including innovative ideas to showcase technology | B. observing user behavior | C. putting oneself in the situation of the end-user | D. creating requirements based on business leaders priorities
column6: Human-centered design (HCD) is fundamentally about understanding and empathizing with users. Observing user behavior (B) provides direct, unfiltered insight into how people actually work, while putting oneself in the user's situation (C) — practicing empathy — ensures design decisions are grounded in real user needs. Showcasing technology (A) is technology-centered, not human-centered. Creating requirements based solely on business leaders' priorities (D) is stakeholder-centered and can overlook actual end-user needs.

column3: Which document should be the source of truth for consistency when implementing a company's brand on Salesforce?
column4: A. Style Guide | B. Design Principles | C. Pattern Library | D. Salesforce Lightning Design System
column6: A company's Style Guide is the authoritative source of truth for brand implementation, defining specific visual standards such as logo usage, color palette, typography, and tone of voice for that particular organization. Design Principles (B) describe broader design philosophy. A Pattern Library (C) contains reusable UI patterns but is not brand-specific. SLDS (D) is Salesforce's own design system and serves as a foundation, but a company's Style Guide customizes and extends it for brand-specific requirements.

column3: A UX Designer is asked to design a new application built on Salesforce.What should be their first step?
column4: A. Create branding sets for each audience using Experience Builder. | B. Find and review relevant AppExchange packages. | C. Become familiar with the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) component blueprints. | D. Design a series of custom web component for the app.
column6: The first step when designing a Salesforce application is to become familiar with the SLDS component blueprints, which provide the design foundation, accessibility standards, and interaction patterns that ensure the application aligns with the Salesforce platform. This prevents redundant work and ensures consistency before exploring AppExchange packages (B) or designing custom components (D). Creating branding sets (A) is a later-stage activity after the application architecture and components are understood.

column3: A UX Designeris analyzing their Experience Cloud site, enabled for Knowledge articles, and is using the Featured Topic component to display content.Which UI configuration should be used to further individualize each featured topic?
column4: A. Add a description displayed onmouse hover further description each topic. | B. Select and upload SVG Icons that represent each featured topic. | C. Select and upload images that represent each featured topic. | D. Add a description under the topic label further describing each topic.
column6: The Featured Topic component in Experience Cloud supports uploading images to visually represent each topic, making them visually distinct and individually recognizable for users. This is the supported out-of-the-box UI configuration for individualizing topics. SVG icons (B) are not supported for this component. Hover descriptions (A) are not a built-in option for this component, and adding text descriptions under labels (D) is not a supported configuration for the Featured Topic component.

column3: Cloud Kicks has identified that users are getting anxious over a multiple-step custom Screen Flow with no way of visualizing its completeness.What should be done to improve the user experience?
column4: A. Replace multiple-step Screen Flow with a static vertical form. | B. Set up a Salesforce Path with Guidance for Success. | C. Configure cascading accordions to condense the experience. | D. Create a Progress Indicator component that displays the flow's stages.
column6: Adding a Progress Indicator component within the Screen Flow gives users a clear visual representation of where they are in the process and how many steps remain, directly addressing the anxiety caused by uncertainty about completion. A static vertical form (A) removes the guided flow benefits. Salesforce Path (B) is for record-stage progression, not multi-step forms. Cascading accordions (C) would condense content but not communicate progress through the flow.

column3: The UX team at Cloud Kicks is examining the user interface of the company's customer-facing portal that runs on Experience Cloud. They want to determine the portal's compliance with recognized standard usability principles.How should this be accomplished?
column4: A. Intuitive Review | B. A Heuristic Evaluation | C. User Testing
column6: A Heuristic Evaluation is the recognized method for assessing a user interface against established usability principles (heuristics), such as Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics. It is an expert review method that evaluates compliance with standards without requiring end-user participants. User Testing (C) involves real users and measures actual behavior rather than standards compliance. 'Intuitive Review' (A) is not a recognized UX methodology.

column3: A UX Designer wants to remotely collect feedback from hundreds of users on tasks or activities that do not require much imagination or emotion.Which testing approach should be used?
column4: A. Online surveys | B. Usability study | C. A/B Testing
column6: Online surveys are ideal for remotely collecting structured feedback at scale from hundreds of users on straightforward tasks or activities that do not require emotional or imaginative responses. Usability studies (B) are typically moderated, observational, and limited in participant scale. A/B Testing (C) compares two design variations to measure behavioral outcomes, not to collect direct user feedback on tasks.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) has an Unlimited Edition Salesforce org. CK's UX Designer has identified that dynamic dashboards could be a useful tool to improve orgusability and experience.How many different dynamic dashboards could they create?
column4: A. Up to 3 | B. Unlimited | C. Up to 20 | D. Up to 10
column6: Salesforce Unlimited Edition supports up to 10 dynamic dashboards per org. Dynamic dashboards display data based on the security settings of the logged-in user, enabling personalized views. Enterprise Edition supports up to 5, and Performance/Unlimited support up to 10. This limit is a key platform constraint UX Designers must understand when planning dashboard-based experiences.

column3: A UX Designer wants to customize the end user's Salesforce app experience.Which administrative capability should be used for mobile navigation menu setup?
column4: A. Menu tabs can be based on different user types. | B. Menu tabs visibility is based on user location. | C. Menu tabs can be Visualforce and Lightning pages.
column6: In the Salesforce mobile navigation menu setup, administrators can include both Visualforce pages and Lightning pages as menu tabs, providing flexibility in what content is surfaced to users. Menu tab visibility based on user location (B) is not a supported capability in Salesforce mobile navigation. While different navigation menus can be configured per app (not per user type directly), this is done at the app level, making option A misleading in this context.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to display contact information, including avatar, name, and title, for people who are related to a customer on a Record page. However, little space is available.Which standard component's design should be used given CK's constraints?
column4: A. Table | B. Tree Grid | C. Tiles | D. Interactive Cards
column6: Tiles are designed to display compact, visual representations of records containing an avatar, name, and additional details like a title, making them ideal for showing related contact information in limited space. Tables (A) are suited for dense tabular data comparisons. Tree Grid (B) is for hierarchical data with nested rows. Interactive Cards (D) are larger format components better suited for more detailed content layouts.

column3: A Ux designer has been asked to improve salesforce adoption among sales representatives at Cloud Kicks after conducting stakeholder and user interviewsthe designers finds there is no clear consistent sale process What should the designer do next
column4: A. Tell the customer they need to improve operations before any work can be done | B. log the findings and move forward with presenting possible solutions | C. recommend field level validation to ensure users are entering the correct data | D. conduct a workshop with stakeholders to align on correct state and build consensus
column6: When research reveals a foundational organizational issue like an inconsistent sales process, the UX Designer's next step is to facilitate a workshop with stakeholders to align on what the correct process should be and build consensus before proposing solutions. This is a core discovery and alignment activity. Jumping to solutions (B) without resolving the underlying process disagreement will produce a design no one agrees on. Field validation (C) addresses data quality but not the process inconsistency. Telling the client to fix operations first (A) is unhelpful and dismissive.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is building a new community portal using Experience Cloud. CK's Designer is asked to provide examples of out-of-box functionality that will make for great customer engagement.In which three Ways Could CK's customers engage?Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Endorsement of skills and expertise | B. Customize page layouts | C. Choose personalized branding | D. Join affinity groups | E. Recognize peers with badges
column6: Experience Cloud provides out-of-the-box community engagement features including endorsing other members' skills and expertise (A), joining topic-based groups or affinity groups (D), and recognizing peers with reputation badges (E) through Chatter and community gamification features. Customizing page layouts (B) is an admin/designer capability, not a customer engagement feature. Personalized branding (C) is a platform configuration capability, not an end-user engagement action.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is incorporating Relationship Design principle into its business model and customer offerings wherever possible. Choose 3 answers
column4: A. Prioritizing Innovation over copying the competition | B. Releasing Salesforce updates in managed packages over unmanaged packages | C. Reframing products in terms of user value over features and functions | D. Prioritize engagement number of impressions | E. Uncovering customer needs over broadcasting product benefits
column6: Relationship Design principles emphasize creating genuine value and authentic connections rather than chasing metrics. Prioritizing innovation over copying competitors (A) reflects a commitment to genuine problem-solving. Reframing products in terms of user value rather than features (C) is a core Relationship Design tenet. Uncovering customer needs rather than broadcasting product benefits (E) reflects a listening-first, empathy-driven approach. Prioritizing engagement metrics like impressions (D) is a performance-marketing mindset that conflicts with authentic relationship building. Package type decisions (B) are technical and irrelevant to Relationship Design.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to create a site for its customers to manage events regardless of the device they are using.Which best practice should be used to provide this experience?
column4: A. Create a responsive user interface. | B. Develop a separate native mobile app for Android and iOS. | C. Launch two different interfaces from the same page.
column6: A responsive user interface adapts its layout to any screen size or device type, providing a consistent experience across desktop, tablet, and mobile without the cost and complexity of maintaining separate apps. Developing separate native apps (B) for each platform is costly and creates maintenance overhead. Launching two different interfaces from the same page (C) creates confusion and is not a recognized UX best practice.

column3: A UX Designer at Cloud Kicks (CK) recommends a Salesforce Console application for CK's service representatives.The service representatives work on multiple support cases hour, accessing them via queues, calls, or live chat.Which console navigation feature would NOT be relevant to the designer recommendation?
column4: A. More than one detail item can be open at a time. | B. Multiple subtabs can be beneath a single parent record. | C. A split list of recordsand individual record detail can see on the same screen. | D. Service representatives with lower resolution monitors will have a better user experience.
column6: Salesforce Console is optimized for high-density workspaces with multiple open tabs and split-screen layouts, which actually requires adequate screen resolution to be effective. Service representatives with lower-resolution monitors would have a worse experience with Console, not a better one, making option D incorrect and therefore the feature NOT relevant to the recommendation. Options A, B, and C are all genuine benefits of Console navigation that support multitasking across cases.

column3: Cloud Kicks is planning its einstein Bot implementation and has identified common issues the Bot can resolve. CK has determined that extensive technical planning is needed for bot effectiveness and customer satisfaction
column4: A. Training and support for planning | B. user interface planning | C. deboarding planning | D. voice and tone planning
column6: User interface planning is essential for Einstein Bot implementations because the bot's UI — how it presents messages, options, quick replies, and escalation paths — directly affects whether customers find the experience intuitive and satisfying. Technical planning alone is insufficient without ensuring the conversational interface is well-designed. Voice and tone planning (D) is important but is typically considered a content/brand exercise rather than a technical planning element for bot effectiveness.

column3: Users from a small group within a sales teams have complained about an object that is often used only by them that has not been a edit to their lighting app due to small volume of users the administrator is not considering a new app for them Which two salesforce features should be suggested to improve the end-user experience ?
column4: A. create a custom component on a dashboard | B. add the objects related list to the home page | C. Favorite the often used object | D. personalized the navigation bar
column6: Since a new app is not being created for this small group, the most practical self-service options are for users to Favorite the often-used object (C) so it is quickly accessible from the Recent Items or navigation, and to personalize their navigation bar (D) to add the object directly to their app navigation without admin intervention. Creating a custom dashboard component (A) does not provide direct object navigation access. Adding a related list to the home page (B) is not a standard Salesforce capability in this context.

column3: W UX wants to customer the end user's Salesforce app experience.Which two administrator capabilities should be used for mobile navigation menu setup?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Tab visibility is dependent on the location of the user.Different menu configurations can be set for different types of users. | B. Navigation items are configure in the Salesforce Navigation Setup mode. | C. Visualforce pages and Lightning pages can be included
column6: In Salesforce mobile navigation setup, administrators configure navigation items using the Salesforce Navigation Setup mode (B), and both Visualforce pages and Lightning pages can be added as navigation items (C). Tab visibility based on user location (A) is not a supported capability in Salesforce mobile navigation — location-based visibility is not a native feature.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants gamified learning content for its internal users and leadership. CK wants to create a custom product training as a part of the experience.What should be recommended?
column4: A. In-app prompts to provide access to video content | B. Sandbox to experiment with the product | C. Enablement Site to build and assign custom modules
column6: Salesforce Enablement Sites (formerly myTrailhead) allow organizations to create custom branded learning modules with gamification elements such as badges and points, which can be assigned to specific users or groups for onboarding and product training. In-app prompts (A) can link to content but cannot host custom training modules. A Sandbox (B) allows hands-on experimentation but does not provide structured gamified learning content.

column3: Which two UX design principles are key to creating excellent mobile user experiences?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Removal of all images for faster load times | B. Increase the need fortyping with the onscreen keyboard | C. Consistency. across device experiences | D. Prioritization of content and UI elements on the screen
column6: Excellent mobile UX depends on maintaining consistency across device experiences (C) so users have a predictable, familiar interface, and on prioritizing which content and UI elements appear on screen (D) given the limited real estate of mobile devices. Removing all images (A) is an extreme measure that harms the experience; optimizing images is more appropriate. Increasing typing requirements (B) is the opposite of good mobile design, as keyboard input on mobile is cumbersome and error-prone.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) is planning its Einstein Bot implementation and has identified common issues the bot canresolve. CK has determined that extensive technical planning is needed for bot effectiveness and customer satisfaction.Which additional element(s) would be essential?
column4: A. User interface planning | B. Voice and tone planning | C. Onboarding planning | D. Training and support planning
column6: Beyond technical planning, user interface planning is essential for Einstein Bot implementations because the way the bot visually presents its conversation — including menus, quick reply buttons, message formatting, and escalation options — directly impacts customer satisfaction and task completion rates. While voice and tone planning (B) is important for content, the question asks for the additional element most essential to bot effectiveness alongside technical planning, which is UI planning.

column3: Cloud Kicks' Sales team needs In-App Guidance for key functions and processes so they can maximize their time.In which way should a UX Designer customize the Salesforce Help Menu to meet this request?
column4: A. Show a site map of all the content. | B. Create a docked prompt based on new feature rollouts. | C. Provide links to external resources, such as training videos or a company dictionary.
column6: [CORRECTED from C to A] The Salesforce Help Menu can be customized to show a site map or custom content structure that helps users navigate to key functions and processes. However, reviewing this question more carefully: the most relevant answer for in-app guidance customization through the Help Menu for key functions is providing links to external resources such as training videos (C). The Help Menu in Salesforce Setup allows admins to add custom links to external training materials, videos, and documentation — directly addressing the need for guidance on key processes. A site map (A) is not a standard Help Menu customization. Docked prompts (B) are part of In-App Guidance but not the Help Menu itself.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to better deliver products to its customers. CK's UX Designer has an innovative solution but is struggling to get the team to visualize where and why it fits into what the customer does.Which technique should be used?
column4: A. Personas | B. Storyboards | C. High-fidelity Prototype
column6: Storyboards use a visual narrative sequence — similar to a comic strip — to show how and where a solution fits into a user's real-world context and workflow. They are particularly effective for helping teams who are not designers visualize the use case and understand why a solution matters in the customer's world. Personas (A) describe who the user is but do not show how a solution fits into their journey. High-fidelity prototypes (C) show detailed UI but require significant effort and do not convey context as naturally as storyboards.

column3: A UX Designer has created two different user interface designs for a new marketing landing expected to haveseveral visitors. The landing page has a contact form on it, and the designer wants to know which design products the most from completions.Which testing method should be used?
column4: A. Card Sorting | B. User Acceptance testing | C. Diary Studies | D. A/B testing
column6: A/B Testing is the ideal method for comparing two design variations with real users at scale to determine which one produces a better measurable outcome — in this case, more form completions. It splits live traffic between the two designs and measures conversion rates objectively. Card sorting (A) tests information architecture. User Acceptance Testing (B) validates that a solution meets requirements before launch. Diary Studies (C) capture self-reported longitudinal user behavior over time.

column3: Cloud Kicks' support agents needto interact with customer information stored in several different objects when handling cases submitted from their web form.In which two ways could the agent experience be made more efficient? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Open links in new windows when agents click on details from case widgets. | B. Use Salesforce Flow to help complete fields required for case closure. | C. Design custom Lightning Web Components for the customer to complete. | D. Complete relevant information about the customer using Lightning Record Pages.
column6: Using Salesforce Flow to guide agents through completing required case closure fields (B) reduces errors and cognitive load by structuring the process. Using Lightning Record Pages to surface related customer information from multiple objects (D) keeps everything in one place, reducing navigation and context switching. Opening links in new windows (A) fragments the workflow and is not an efficiency improvement. Building custom LWCs for customers to complete (C) is a customer-facing solution, not an agent efficiency improvement.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to apply branding to its current Salesforce org, currently using Lightning Experience. The look and feel must follow company design guidelines.Which declarative design properties should be used to achieve this?
column4: A. Create a custom Theme in Themes and Branding, selecting logo, brand colors, and images. | B. Design a custom Branding set in use interface, selecting a logo,colors, and font type. | C. Develop a custom Layout in user interface, attaching a new stylesheet in static resources. | D. Choose one of the built-in Salesforce themes that closest matches the design guidelines
column6: The Themes and Branding feature in Salesforce Setup allows administrators to declaratively create and apply custom themes by selecting logos, brand colors, and imagery — all without writing code. This is the standard declarative approach for branding a Salesforce Lightning Experience org. Branding sets (B) are specific to Experience Cloud, not the core Lightning org. Attaching a custom stylesheet via static resources (C) is a code-based approach, not declarative. Choosing a built-in theme (D) would not match custom company design guidelines.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to represent stages for opportunities within the sales process.Which Salesforce Lightning Designing System (SLDS) component should be recommended?
column4: A. Scoped Tabs | B. Activity Timeline | C. Progress indicator | D. Path
column6: The Path component in SLDS is specifically designed to visually represent stages in a business process, such as opportunity stages in a sales process. It displays the current stage highlighted along a horizontal progression, with guidance and key fields for each stage. Progress Indicator (C) shows completion of steps in a sequential process but is more generic and does not include stage-specific guidance. Scoped Tabs (A) and Activity Timeline (B) are not designed for representing process stages.

column3: Which goal would be important in undertaking a Consequence Scanning exercise before launching a new product?
column4: A. Reframe the product objectives from multiple, diverse perspectives. | B. Encourage team well-being through collaboration. | C. Identify the best angle for positive launch press coverage.
column6: Consequence Scanning is a responsible innovation practice where teams examine the intended and unintended consequences of a product or feature from multiple perspectives — including marginalized or underrepresented groups — before launch. The goal is to reframe product objectives by considering diverse viewpoints to avoid harm and improve inclusivity. It is not a team wellness exercise (B) or a marketing strategy (C).

column3: Service agents are complaining that the new custom object to track reservation has too many fields and is cluttering their layouts. All of the fields are necessary, but they would like to display fields and sections of the record as individual components on the page layout with visibility depending on where they are in the reservation process.Which feature should be recommended?
column4: A. AppExchange Apps | B. Dynamic Forms | C. Process Builder | D. In-App Prompts
column6: Dynamic Forms allows fields and field sections to be placed as individual components anywhere on a Lightning Record Page and configured with visibility rules based on record field values — such as the current stage of the reservation process. This directly solves the problem of too many fields cluttering the layout by showing only relevant fields at each stage. Process Builder (C) is a workflow automation tool. In-App Prompts (D) are contextual guidance overlays. AppExchange Apps (A) are third-party solutions that add functionality rather than address layout management.

column3: A UX Design team is doing a review of a new Lightning Web Component (LWC). They are following the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) block, element, modifier (BEM) naming conventions.Which class does NOT follow this convention?
column4: A. .slds-avatar_circle | B. .slds-box | C. .slds--size-l-of-2
column6: In SLDS BEM naming convention, modifiers are denoted by a single underscore (e.g., .slds-avatar_circle) not a double hyphen. The class .slds--size-l-of-2 uses a double hyphen which is not the SLDS BEM convention — SLDS uses a single hyphen for block-element separation and a single underscore for modifiers. Therefore .slds--size-l-of-2 violates the convention. .slds-avatar_circle (A) correctly uses the modifier pattern, and .slds-box (B) is a valid block-level class.

column3: Cloud Kicks has a content-rich set of record pages and wants its UX Design team to organize and consolidate them.Which Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) component should be used to organize and consolidate content?
column4: A. Data Tables | B. Modals | C. Q Tabs
column6: Tabs (the 'Q Tabs' option refers to Tabs/Scoped Tabs in SLDS) are designed specifically to organize and consolidate content-rich pages by grouping related content into separate tabbed sections, reducing cognitive load and page clutter. Data Tables (A) are for displaying structured tabular data, not organizing page sections. Modals (B) are overlay dialogs for focused tasks, not a persistent page organization tool.

column3: Universal Containers (UC) operates worldwide with offices in more than 100 regions in 10 different countries role hierarchy to control data visibility. In the new fiscal year, UC is planned to reorganize the roles and reassign accounts owners. Which two points should an architect consider in this situation?Which two point should an Architect consider in this situation?Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Using a temporary parking lot account to improve performance. | B. Changing complex role hierarchy can cause a high level of sharing recalculation. | C. Restricting the organization-sharing configurations to private. | D. Replacing Account records ownerships massively can cause data skew.
column6: When reorganizing a complex role hierarchy in Salesforce, changing the role hierarchy structure triggers extensive sharing rule recalculations (B), which can impact system performance significantly in large orgs with many records. Massively reassigning Account record ownership (D) can cause data skew — where a single user owns a disproportionate number of records — which degrades performance in related list queries and sharing calculations. These are two critical architectural considerations for this type of reorganization.

column3: A UX Designer is asked to design a responsive page. When screen resolution changes, the content of the page should expand across columns or wrap and push itself onto new rows.Which Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS) utility provides the most flexible system to meet these requirements?
column4: A. Spacing | B. Layout | C. Alignment | D. Grid
column6: The SLDS Grid utility provides a flexible, responsive CSS grid system based on flexbox that allows content to expand across columns at larger screen sizes and wrap onto new rows at smaller sizes. It is the foundation for building responsive layouts in Salesforce. Spacing utilities (A) control margins and padding, Alignment utilities (C) control element positioning, and Layout utilities (B) are higher-level containers — none provide the column-wrapping responsive behavior that Grid does.

column3: Cloud Kicks (CK) wants to integrate learning in the flow of work and is considering using In-App Learning functionality. CK wants to assign learning content to employees as part of its onboarding process.Which two features could be assigned to learners? Choose 2 answers
column4: A. Trailmixes | B. Trails | C. Modules/Badges | D. External LMS Content
column6: Salesforce In-App Learning (Enablement) supports assigning Trailmixes (A) — curated collections of Trailhead content — and individual Modules/Badges (C) directly to learners as part of structured onboarding programs. Trails (B) are pre-built Trailhead learning paths that are not directly assignable through the Enablement feature in the same way. External LMS Content (D) is not natively assignable through Salesforce's In-App Learning feature without integration.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to prevent its sales agents from being able to save a new account unless they have entered the phone number in the correct format and with the correct number of digits. A validation rule would then prevent the new account from saving.Which method should be used to improve the user experience in the simplest way while preventing errors?
column4: A. Set a prompt to display on the page using In-App Guidance. | B. Set field-level error message to display on the page. | C. Mark the field as Required.
column6: Setting a field-level error message to display on the page — delivered through the validation rule's error message configured directly on the field — provides inline, contextual feedback that tells the user exactly what is wrong and where, without requiring them to search for the issue. This is the simplest UX improvement because it works in conjunction with the existing validation rule. In-App Guidance prompts (A) are general guidance tools, not inline field validation feedback. Marking the field as Required (C) only enforces that data is entered, not that it matches a specific format.

column3: It is recommended to carefully consider which demographic data and for what purpose is fed into an AI model.Which reason explains this?
column4: A. To avoid societal bias | B. To avoid unconscious confirmation bias | C. To avoid interaction bias
column6: Demographic data fed into AI models can encode and amplify existing societal biases — for example, historical data reflecting past discrimination in hiring, lending, or healthcare. If such data is used without careful consideration, the AI model will perpetuate and potentially scale those biases, causing harm to underrepresented groups. This is a societal bias concern, which is the primary reason for scrutinizing demographic inputs to AI systems.

column3: Which criteria should the designer consider when selecting users for testing?
column4: A. Traits dissimilar to customer personas | B. Traits similar to friends and family | C. Traits similar to customer personas | D. traits of the most common demographic and ability
column6: Test participants should be selected based on traits that match the target customer personas to ensure that test results reflect the actual intended users' behavior, mental models, and pain points. Testing with users who share traits with friends and family (B) introduces convenience sampling bias. Testing only the most common demographic (D) excludes edge cases and diverse users. Testing users dissimilar to personas (A) produces irrelevant findings.

column3: The Client Service team at Cloud Kicks is having issues when editing an order record. The validation rule returns a top-of-page error indicating two other fields must be provided to validate and save the order changes.How should they efficiently find the correct fields?
column4: A. Make sure the required field names are added to the top-of-page error message. | B. Replace the validation rule with help text informing the businessrequirement. | C. Replace the top-of-page validation with two field validations, assigning the respective fields. | D. Remove the validation rule completely and provide training about order changes.
column6: Replacing the single top-of-page validation error with individual field-level validation errors assigned directly to the respective fields (C) ensures that the error messages appear inline next to the specific fields that need attention, making it immediately obvious to users which fields require input. A generic top-of-page message naming the fields (A) is better but still requires users to search the page. Help text (B) provides guidance but cannot prevent saving without a validation rule. Removing the rule (D) eliminates the business control entirely.

column3: Cloud Kicks is making inclusive design a priority for its communities and customers.What are the three inclusive design action-oriented principles?
column4: A. Recognize imperfection. Learn from diversity. One size fits one. | B. Recognize exclusion. Learn from diversity. Solve for one, extend to many. | C. Recognize diversity. Learn from experts. Focus on one person.
column6: The three Microsoft-originated inclusive design principles — widely adopted in the UX and Salesforce community — are: Recognize exclusion (identify where and how people are being excluded), Learn from diversity (use diverse human experiences as insights to drive innovation), and Solve for one, extend to many (solutions designed for people with specific constraints benefit everyone). Options A and C contain incorrect or paraphrased versions of these principles.

column3: Service agents are complaining that the new custom object to track reservations has too many fields and is cluttering their layouts. All of the fields are necessary, but they would like to display fields and sections of the record as individual components on the page layout with visibility depending on where they are in the reservation process.Which feature should be recommended? [{"voted_answers": "A", "vote_count": 1, "is_most_voted": false}]
column4: A. Flow Builder | B. In-App Guidance | C. Dynamic Forms
column6: Dynamic Forms is the correct feature for this scenario because it allows fields and sections from the record's page layout to be placed as individual components anywhere on a Lightning Record Page and configured with conditional visibility rules based on field values — such as the current stage of the reservation. This is a near-identical scenario to question #159. Flow Builder (A) automates processes but does not control field display on a record page. In-App Guidance (B) provides overlays and prompts to guide users, not field-level visibility control.

column3: Cloud Kicks wants to plan out the strategy for an upcoming discovery phase. Which three practices should be considered? Choose 3 answers The discovery phase is a crucial stage in any UX design project, as it helps to understand the user needs, business goals, and technical constraints of the problem1.The discovery phase typically involves various research methods, such as interviews, surveys, observations, and analytics, to gather data and insights about the users and the context of use2. The outputs of the discovery phase are artifacts that synthesize and communicate the findings and insights from the research. Some of the practices that should be considered for planning out the strategy for an upcoming discovery phase are: Gather insight from end users: One of the main objectives of the discovery phase is to empathize with the end users and understand their goals, motivations, pain points, behaviors, and preferences.This can be done by conducting user research, such as interviews, surveys, focus groups, or usability tests, to collect qualitative and quantitative data from the target audience3. Gathering insight from end users helps to validate the assumptions and hypotheses about the problem and the solution, as well as to identify the user requirements and expectations for the design. Establish the research plan and timeline: Before conducting any user research, it is important to establish a clear and realistic research plan and timeline. The research plan should define the research objectives, questions, methods, participants, and deliverables.The research timeline should specify the duration, frequency, and sequence of the research activities, as well as the deadlines and milestones for the deliverables4. Establishing the research plan and timeline helps to ensure that the discovery phase is well-organized, efficient, and effective, as well as to communicate the expectations and responsibilities to the stakeholders and the team members. Understand the problem before moving to solutions: Another key practice for the discovery phase is to focus on understanding the problem before jumping to solutions. This means defining the problem statement, the scope, and the constraints of the project, as well as identifying the root causes, the symptoms, and the impacts of the problem. Understanding the problem before moving to solutions helps to avoid wasting time and resources on creating solutions that do not address the real needs and pain points of the users, or that are not feasible or viable for the business or the technology. The other two options, considering platform-based before custom solutions and determining user acceptance criteria, are not practices that should be considered for planning out the strategy for an upcoming discovery phase.Considering platform-based before custom solutions is a practice that belongs to the design phase, not the discovery phase, as it involves choosing the best solution option based on the user research findings and the design principles5.Determining user acceptance criteria is a practice that belongs to the testing phase, not the discovery phase, as it involves defining the criteria that the solution must meet to be accepted by the users and the stakeholders6. For Cloud Kicks' upcoming discovery phase, the three practices that should be considered are: A) Understand the Problem Before Moving to Solutions: Establishing a clear understanding of theproblem and what the desired outcomes are before beginning the discovery phase is essential for successful project planning. This can be done by gathering data and insights from end users, conducting research, and understanding how the problem is currentlybeing addressed. B) Gather Insight from End Users: Gathering insights from end users is a crucial step in the discovery phase. End users provide valuable feedback and insights into the problem and how a potential solution may work. This feedback can helpshape the overall project plan and help identify potential solutions. C) Establish the Research Plan and Timeline: The research plan and timeline should be established before beginning the discovery phase. This should include a list of tasks to be completed, the resources needed, and a timeline for completion. This plan should be communicated to all stakeholders so everyone is aware of the project goals and timeline.
column4: A. Consider platform-based before custom solutions. | B. Gather insight from end users. | C. Establish the research plan and timeline. | D. Understand the problem before moving to solutions. | E. Determine user acceptance criteria.
column6: The three correct discovery phase practices are: Gather insight from end users (B) to build empathy and understand real needs; Establish the research plan and timeline (C) to ensure organized, purposeful research activities; and Understand the problem before moving to solutions (D) to avoid solution bias and ensure the right problem is being addressed. Considering platform-based vs. custom solutions (A) belongs to the design/build phase, not discovery. Determining user acceptance criteria (E) belongs to the testing phase. These three principles reflect the human-centered discovery methodology used in Salesforce UX practice.
